7C6C DEFB $20,$80,$82,$80,$40,$08,$81,$80

7CC4 DEFB $81,$B1,$B1,$B1,$81,$81,$81,$B1
7CCC DEFB $B1,$81,$50,$81,$40,$60,$81,$08

7CE4 DEFB $83,$83,$83,$81,$83,$83,$81,$83
7CEC DEFB $81,$83,$83,$81,$40,$83,$83,$83

7CFC DEFB $83,$81,$83,$81,$83,$83,$81,$83

7D0C DEFB $50,$83,$83,$85,$83,$83,$70,$83

7D1C DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$08,$81,$81,$81
7D24 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$20,$30,$80,$85,$80
7D2C DEFB $80,$80,$40,$80,$40,$81,$08,$C3
7D34 DEFB $81,$C3,$81,$C3,$81,$81,$81,$81
7D3C DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7D44 DEFB $85,$80,$85,$50,$85,$80,$82,$80
7D4C DEFB $81,$08,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7D54 DEFB $85,$40,$81,$81,$08,$85,$81,$81
7D5C DEFB $08,$40,$81,$50,$83,$83,$82,$83

7DBC DEFB $81,$50,$81,$81,$08,$81,$82,$81

7DCC DEFB $60,$81,$60,$81,$C3,$C3,$C3,$81
7DD4 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$85,$85
7DDC DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$91,$81,$81
7DE4 DEFB $91,$81,$81,$85,$81,$60,$85,$08
7DEC DEFB $50,$50,$0C,$81,$91,$82,$20,$40
7DF4 DEFB $08,$81,$81,$91,$08,$20,$81,$81
7DFC DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7E04 DEFB $81,$08,$81,$81,$85,$82,$82,$60
7E0C DEFB $81,$85,$60,$08,$81,$85,$60,$08
7E14 DEFB $08,$81,$81,$91,$91,$81,$91,$91
7E1C DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7E24 DEFB $85,$40,$60,$81,$81,$08,$81,$81
7E2C DEFB $81,$85,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7E34 DEFB $81,$70,$08,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7E3C DEFB $81,$81,$81,$85,$85,$85,$85,$40

7E5C DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$20,$20,$81,$91
7E64 DEFB $08,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$20
7E6C DEFB $20,$08,$C3,$C3,$C3,$81,$81,$81
7E74 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$40,$85

7E84 DEFB $81,$81,$82,$81,$81,$30,$30,$81

7E94 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$83,$83,$83,$83,$81
7E9C DEFB $81,$83,$81,$81,$08,$83,$83,$81
7EA4 DEFB $83,$83,$81,$83,$83,$81,$81,$83
7EAC DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$85,$40,$40,$81
7EB4 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$40,$85,$85,$81,$81
7EBC DEFB $40,$81,$08,$82,$81,$81,$81,$82
7EC4 DEFB $81,$08,$83,$81,$81,$81,$83,$83
7ECC DEFB $83,$83,$85,$83,$81,$83,$83,$83
7ED4 DEFB $91,$83,$81,$83,$83,$91,$91,$91

7EE4 DEFB $81,$81,$83,$83,$10,$81,$83,$83
7EEC DEFB $83,$83,$08,$81,$81,$91,$81,$81
7EF4 DEFB $10,$81,$91,$91,$81,$81,$81,$81
7EFC DEFB $81,$81,$0C,$85,$70,$81,$81,$81
7F04 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7F0C DEFB $08,$81,$81,$81,$85,$81,$82,$81
7F14 DEFB $82,$81,$81,$81,$60,$08,$81,$81

7F24 DEFB $81,$40,$81,$83,$81,$83,$82,$83
7F2C DEFB $81,$83,$81,$81,$83,$81,$83,$81
7F34 DEFB $40,$81,$81,$82,$81,$91,$81,$81

7F4C DEFB $83,$83,$C3,$83,$83,$83,$83,$83
7F54 DEFB $83,$83,$C3,$83,$83,$00,$00,$00
7F5C DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F64 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F6C DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F74 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F7C DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F84 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F8C DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F94 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F9C DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FA4 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00

7FCC DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FD4 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FDC DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FE4 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FEC DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FF4 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FFC DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00
8000: GAME ENTRY -- cold-start, reached from the resident loader.
The loader JP's here once the tape image is fully in RAM.
In: nothing meaningful (IFF1=0, IM 1, SP still loader's).
Out: never returns -- falls through into the init chain and then the main game loop.
Side effects: installs private stack, blacks the border, runs three init subroutines, seeds game-state RAM.
game_entry 8000 JP entry_setup alignment trampoline -- skip 3 pad bytes (8003-8005)
8003 NOP
freeze_ctr 8004 NOP
pad_var_8005 8005 NOP
entry_setup 8006 LD SP,$5FFE install game's private stack just under screen RAM
8009 XOR A A = 0
800A OUT ($FE),A ULA port FE: border=black(0), MIC/beeper off (i8080: OUT to ULA-equiv)
800C CALL setup_im2 init #1 -- install IM 2 interrupt vector (see setup_im2)
800F CALL jp_clear_screen init #2 = clear screen + game RAM (jp_clear_screen trampoline)
8012 CALL $A71E init #3 = paint playfield attributes (jp_setup_attrs trampoline)
8015 LD A,$01 A = 1
8017 LD ($603F),A first persistent-state write: running_flag = 1
801A: NEW_GAME -- set up a fresh game/level and run it.
Falls in from game_entry's init chain.
Seeds state vars, builds the level, draws the screen, then enters main_loop.
On death/level-end it loops back to level_retry or game-over handling.
new_game 801A LD A,$80 A=$80 -> $6056 (start flag/counter)
801C LD ($6056),A
801F XOR A
8020 LD ($60D7),A
8023 LD ($8005),A
8026 LD A,$04 4 attempts/lives -> $6017
8028 LD ($6017),A
802B: Routine at 802B
Used by the routine at poll_controls.
level_retry 802B XOR A
802C LD ($60D8),A
802F CALL title_menu show title screen / menu (waits for start)
This entry point is used by the routine at poll_controls.
level_retry_0 8032 CALL init_col_state seed per-player column-state words
8035 CALL jp_reset_score proc_table slot: build tower / level layout
8038 LD A,$01
803A LD ($6040),A
This entry point is used by the routine at main_loop.
level_retry_1 803D CALL restore_col_state restore_col_state (CY = success)
8040 PUSH AF
8041 CALL jp_init_bonus
8044 POP AF
8045 JR NC,level_retry carry clear -> rebuild (retry)
This entry point is used by the routine at main_loop.
level_retry_2 8047 CALL jp_set_level_timer
804A CALL jp_draw_timer
804D CALL jp_draw_hud
8050 CALL code_9963 draw the heading/menu text
8053 XOR A
8054 LD ($60C3),A
8057 DEC A
8058 LD ($6055),A
805B CALL jp_clear_playfield
805E CALL setup_level
8061 CALL stop_sound
8064: MAIN_LOOP -- the in-play frame loop.
Each pass runs main_tick (one frame) then checks end conditions: death_flag ($60C3) set -> life-loss handler ($8084) game_state ($60D9) == 2 -> level-complete ($80B2) play_timer ($6053) == 0 -> time-up ($8081) Otherwise CALL $8115 (status update) and loop.
main_loop 8064 CALL main_tick run one frame of the game
8067 LD A,($60C3) player died this frame?
806A OR A
806B JR NZ,on_death yes -> life-loss handler
806D LD A,($60D9) level-complete state?
8070 CP $02
8072 JR Z,on_level_done yes -> level-complete handler
8074 LD HL,($6053) play_timer reached 0?
8077 LD A,H
8078 OR L
8079 JR Z,main_loop_0 yes -> time-up
807B CALL poll_controls per-frame status/HUD update
807E JP main_loop loop
main_loop_0 8081 CALL draw_timeup_screen time-up handler
on_death 8084 LD A,$FF
8086 LD ($6055),A
8089 LD A,($60D7)
808C OR A
808D JR NZ,main_loop_2
808F LD HL,$6041
8092 LD A,($6040)
8095 OR A
8096 JR Z,main_loop_1
8098 INC HL
main_loop_1 8099 DEC (HL)
809A PUSH HL
809B CALL jp_draw_hud
809E POP HL
809F LD A,(HL)
80A0 OR A
80A1 JR Z,main_loop_3
main_loop_2 80A3 CALL pack_col_state
80A6 JP level_retry_1
main_loop_3 80A9 CALL clear_col_mask
80AC CALL draw_info_screen_a
80AF JP level_retry_1
on_level_done 80B2 LD A,$FF
80B4 LD ($6055),A
80B7 LD A,($60B9)
80BA PUSH AF
80BB LD HL,($60BA)
80BE PUSH HL
80BF LD HL,($6030)
80C2 PUSH HL
80C3 CALL screen_time_bonus
80C6 CALL jp_clear_playfield
80C9 LD A,$03
80CB LD ($60D9),A
80CE CALL setup_level
80D1 POP HL
80D2 LD ($6030),HL
80D5 POP HL
80D6 LD ($60BA),HL
80D9 POP AF
80DA LD ($60B9),A
80DD LD A,$04
80DF LD ($60C0),A
80E2 LD A,$1D
80E4 LD ($60BF),A
80E7 XOR A
80E8 LD ($60C2),A
main_loop_4 80EB CALL main_tick
80EE CALL poll_controls
80F1 LD A,($60D9)
80F4 CP $04
80F6 JR NZ,main_loop_4
80F8 XOR A
80F9 LD ($60D9),A
80FC CALL advance_col_counter
80FF CP $08
8101 JR NZ,main_loop_5
8103 CALL clear_col_mask
8106 CALL draw_info_screen_b
8109 JP level_retry_1
main_loop_5 810C CALL pack_col_state
810F CALL jp_init_bonus
8112 JP level_retry_2
8115: POLL_CONTROLS -- per-frame keyboard handling (called from main_loop).
Used by the routine at main_loop.
Skipped while input is frozen (freeze_ctr).
- restart key (via $A70F): NC -> jump to level_retry
- several control keys toggle ctrl_flag ($60D7) / pad_var_8005
- PAUSE/DEMO: key $21 enters demo_pause any joystick X exits
- menu select: tests a column of 8 keys the index of the one pressed (0..7) -> pad_var_8005, then restarts setup at $8032.
Uses test_key ($A706) for t
poll_controls 8115 LD A,($8004) A = freeze_ctr
8118 OR A
8119 RET NZ frozen -> do nothing this frame
811A CALL jp_test_break
811D JP NC,level_retry restart condition -> rebuild level
8120 LD A,$27
8122 CALL jp_test_key
8125 JR Z,poll_controls_0
8127 LD A,$08
8129 CALL jp_test_key
812C JR Z,poll_controls_0
812E LD A,$15
8130 CALL jp_test_key
8133 JR Z,poll_controls_0
8135 LD A,$01
8137 LD ($60D7),A set ctrl_flag ($60D7) = 1
poll_controls_0 813A LD A,$20
813C CALL jp_test_key
813F JR Z,poll_controls_1
8141 XOR A
8142 LD ($60D7),A
8145 LD ($8005),A
poll_controls_1 8148 LD A,($60D8) A = demo_pause
814B OR A
814C JR NZ,poll_controls_2
814E LD A,($6055)
8151 OR A
8152 JR NZ,poll_controls_4
8154 LD A,$21
8156 CALL jp_test_key
8159 JR Z,poll_controls_4
815B LD A,$FF enter demo/pause mode (demo_pause = $FF)
815D LD ($60D8),A
8160 LD ($6055),A
8163 JR poll_controls_4
poll_controls_2 8165 CALL proc_table demo active: read joystick
8168 LD A,($601C)
816B OR A
816C JR Z,poll_controls_4 no X movement -> stay in demo
816E XOR A
816F LD ($60D8),A movement -> exit demo
8172 LD A,($60D7)
8175 OR A
8176 JP M,poll_controls_3
8179 LD A,$01
poll_controls_3 817B LD ($6055),A
poll_controls_4 817E LD A,($60D7)
8181 OR A
8182 RET Z
8183 LD A,$27
8185 CALL jp_test_key
8188 RET Z
8189 LD B,$00 --- menu key-column scan: B = option index ---
818B LD A,$24
818D CALL jp_test_key
8190 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
8192 INC B
8193 LD A,$1C
8195 CALL jp_test_key test each option key
8198 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
819A INC B
819B LD A,$14
819D CALL jp_test_key
81A0 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81A2 INC B
81A3 LD A,$0C
81A5 CALL jp_test_key
81A8 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81AA INC B
81AB LD A,$04
81AD CALL jp_test_key
81B0 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81B2 INC B
81B3 LD A,$03
81B5 CALL jp_test_key
81B8 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81BA INC B
81BB LD A,$0B
81BD CALL jp_test_key
81C0 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81C2 INC B
81C3 LD A,$13
81C5 CALL jp_test_key
81C8 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81CA RET none pressed -> return
poll_controls_5 81CB LD A,B a key matched: B = selected option
81CC LD ($8005),A store selection -> pad_var_8005
81CF POP HL
81D0 XOR A
81D1 LD ($60D8),A
81D4 JP level_retry_0 restart game setup with the new selection
81D7: SETUP_LEVEL -- (re)build the current level's working state.
Used by the routines at level_retry and main_loop.
Zeroes the work_buffers block ($F000.., $0FFF bytes via LDIR), loads the level's column data (load_level_col), resets the tower position (init_tower_pos), re-inits flags (init_b5cf) and the renderers ($8FB7, $96D4).
Clobbers: most.
setup_level 81D7 LD HL,$F000
81DA LD DE,$F001
81DD LD BC,$0FFF
81E0 LD (HL),$00
81E2 LDIR
81E4 CALL jp_load_level_col
81E7 CALL init_tower_pos
81EA CALL jp_init_b5cf
81ED CALL clear_actor_states
81F0 CALL reset_actor_phase
81F3 LD B,$04
81F5 LD HL,$60A9
setup_level_0 81F8 LD (HL),$FF
81FA INC HL
81FB DJNZ setup_level_0
81FD LD A,($60D9)
8200 CP $03
8202 JR Z,setup_level_1
8204 CALL jp_paint_attrs
8207 LD A,$01
8209 LD ($60C9),A
820C LD A,$24
820E LD HL,$60B9
8211 SUB (HL)
8212 AND $7F
8214 LD ($6032),A
8217 CALL jp_col_visible
821A CALL init_attract_actors
821D LD HL,$60C9
8220 DEC (HL)
8221 XOR A
8222 LD ($60D9),A
8225 LD A,$7D
8227 LD ($60A3),A
822A LD A,$01
822C LD ($60A4),A
822F LD A,($60D7)
8232 JP setup_level_2
setup_level_1 8235 LD A,$FF
setup_level_2 8237 LD ($6055),A
823A LD A,$02
823C LD ($8004),A
823F RET
8240: MAIN_TICK -- one frame of the game.
Used by the routine at main_loop.
Called from main_loop.
Order: read input (unless frozen) -> run game-state machine (player move, tower rotation, enemies) -> redraw widgets -> HALT to sync to the 50Hz frame interrupt.
State: game_state ($60D9): 3=level transition, 4=finished,
else = normal play.
demo_pause ($60D8) skips logic.
freeze_ctr ($8004) freezes input for N frames.
Clobbers: most regs.
Returns once per frame.
i8080 PORT:
main_tick 8240 LD HL,$8004 HL -> freeze_ctr (in code padding at $8004)
8243 LD A,(HL) A = freeze counter
8244 OR A
8245 JR Z,main_tick_0 0 = not frozen -> read input normally
8247 DEC (HL) frozen: count down one frame
8248 XOR A
8249 LD ($601D),A zero the three input vars while frozen
824C LD ($601E),A
824F LD ($601C),A
8252 JR main_tick_1
main_tick_0 8254 CALL proc_table proc_table[0] -> read keyboard/joystick
main_tick_1 8257 LD A,($60D8) A = demo_pause flag
825A OR A
825B JR NZ,main_tick_3 set -> skip all game logic (attract/demo), just redraw
825D LD A,($60D9) A = game_state
8260 CP $03 state 3 = level transition?
8262 JR NZ,main_tick_2
8264 CALL player_tick run transition step
8267 LD A,($60D9)
826A CP $04
826C RET Z state 4 = finished -> done this frame
826D JP main_tick_3
main_tick_2 8270 CALL jp_update_objects --- normal play: update one frame ---
8273 CALL update_actor_pos update actor position around/along the tower (update_actor_pos)
8276 CALL player_tick advance level logic
8279 LD A,($60A4) A = player column before move
827C PUSH AF
827D CALL jp_update_actors move player (proc_table slot)
8280 POP AF
8281 LD B,A
8282 LD A,($60A4) compare new vs old player column
8285 CP B
8286 LD A,$00
8288 CALL NZ,play_sound column changed -> CALL $A3DE (redraw player cell)
828B CALL update_enemies update enemies/hazards
828E CALL check_collision collision + scoring
main_tick_3 8291 LD A,$24 --- redraw HUD: A = $24 (36 rows) ---
8293 LD HL,$60B9
8296 SUB (HL) subtract tower_height ($60B9)
8297 AND $7F
8299 LD ($6032),A store display height ($6032)
829C CALL jp_col_visible draw widgets (proc_table slots)
829F CALL jp_anim_bg
82A2 CALL jp_setup_scroll
82A5 CALL jp_code_ad00
82A8 PUSH AF
82A9 CALL draw_player draw the player sprite
82AC HALT HALT: wait for the 50Hz frame IRQ (Z80
82AD CALL sound_tick post-frame: flip buffers / housekeeping
82B0 CALL jp_draw_tower
82B3 CALL jp_draw_pending
82B6 CALL draw_actors_sorted
82B9 CALL jp_select_sprite
82BC CALL jp_draw_strip
82BF POP AF
82C0 CALL jp_setup_strip
82C3 CALL jp_blit_scroll_wrap
82C6 CALL jp_scroll_attrs
82C9 LD A,($60D9)
82CC CP $03
82CE JR Z,main_tick_4
82D0 CALL tally_score_to
82D3 CALL jp_draw_hud
82D6 CALL jp_tick_timer
main_tick_4 82D9 RET
82DA: TALLY_SCORE_TO -- count $60BC up toward $60BA, awarding $10 (BCD) points per step via add_bcd_score, until the two 16-bit values meet.
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Used to animate an end-of-section bonus tally.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
tally_score_to 82DA LD HL,($60BC)
82DD LD DE,($60BA)
82E1 OR A
82E2 SBC HL,DE
82E4 RET NC
tally_score_to_0 82E5 LD HL,($60BC)
82E8 LD DE,($60BA)
82EC LD A,L
82ED CP E
82EE JR NZ,tally_score_to_1
82F0 LD A,H
82F1 CP D
82F2 RET Z
tally_score_to_1 82F3 PUSH HL
82F4 LD HL,$0010
82F7 CALL jp_add_score
82FA POP HL
82FB INC HL
82FC LD ($60BC),HL
82FF JR tally_score_to_0
8301: Unused
col_state_words 8301 DEFS $04
8305: INIT_COL_STATE -- seed BOTH players' saved column-mask words.
Used by the routine at level_retry.
HL = (pad_var_8005) [low=0 at level start], then H forced to $FF (all 8 columns present) the same $FF00|sel is written to the P1 ($8301) and P2 ($8303) slots.
Called once per level build.
In: pad_var_8005.
Out: ($8301),($8303).
Clobbers: A,HL.
init_col_state 8305 LD HL,($8005)
8308 LD H,$FF
830A LD ($8301),HL
830D LD ($8303),HL
8310 RET
8311: RESTORE_COL_STATE -- expand a player's saved column bitmask into the 8-byte col_flags[] array ($60CB) and mirror its counter into $6039.
Used by the routine at level_retry.
lives_p1 ($6040) selects the slot (0=P1 word $8301, else P2 word $8303) -- here $6040 doubles as an ACTIVE-PLAYER INDEX, not a life count.
running_flag gates an alternate (re)init path.
Unpacks via RR through col_flags[] returns CY=1 on success.
Out: col_flags[0..7], $6039, CY.
Clobbers: A,B,C,H
restore_col_state 8311 LD A,($603F)
8314 CP $01
8316 LD A,$00
8318 LD HL,$6040
831B JR Z,restore_col_state_0
831D INC A
831E SUB (HL)
restore_col_state_0 831F LD (HL),A
8320 LD HL,$8302
8323 OR A
8324 JR Z,restore_col_state_1
8326 INC HL
8327 INC HL
restore_col_state_1 8328 LD A,(HL)
8329 OR A

8360: PACK_COL_STATE -- inverse of restore_col_state.
Used by the routine at main_loop.
Writes the live counter ($6039) into the selected player's low byte, then packs the 8 col_flags[] LSBs ($60CB..$60D2) back into the high mask byte.
lives_p1 selects the P1/P2 slot.
Clobbers: A,B,C,HL.
pack_col_state 8360 LD HL,$8301
8363 LD A,($6040)
8366 OR A
8367 JR Z,pack_col_state_0
8369 INC HL
836A INC HL
pack_col_state_0 836B LD A,($6039)
836E LD (HL),A
836F INC HL
8370 PUSH HL
8371 LD B,$08
8373 XOR A
8374 LD HL,$60CB
pack_col_state_1 8377 LD C,(HL)
8378 RR C
837A RR A
837C INC HL
837D DJNZ pack_col_state_1
837F POP HL
8380 LD (HL),A
8381 RET
8382: CLEAR_COL_MASK -- zero the selected player's column mask byte (high byte of $8301/$8303).
Used by the routine at main_loop.
lives_p1 selects the slot.
clear_col_mask 8382 LD HL,$8302
8385 LD A,($6040)
8388 OR A
8389 JR Z,clear_col_mask_0
838B INC HL
838C INC HL
clear_col_mask_0 838D LD (HL),$00
838F RET
8390: ADVANCE_COL_COUNTER -- mark current column done: clear col_flags[$6039], then bump the selected player's saved counter (low byte) and mirror it back to $6039.
Used by the routine at main_loop.
advance_col_counter 8390 LD A,($6039)
8393 LD E,A
8394 LD D,$00
8396 LD HL,$60CB
8399 ADD HL,DE
839A LD (HL),$00
839C LD HL,$8301
839F LD A,($6040)
83A2 OR A
83A3 JR Z,advance_col_counter_0
83A5 INC HL
83A6 INC HL
advance_col_counter_0 83A7 INC (HL)
83A8 LD A,(HL)
83A9 LD ($6039),A
83AC RET
83AD: SETUP_IM2 -- install the Z80 mode-2 interrupt vector.
Used by the routine at game_entry.
Builds a 257-byte page of identical bytes at $BB00..$BC00 so that, whatever the data-bus byte is at IRQ time, the Z80 reads the 16-bit vector $BABA from table[$FF]/table[$100].
At $BABA it plants 'JP $83CA' -- the actual interrupt service routine.
In: nothing.
Out: IM 2 active, IFF1=1 (interrupts enabled).
Clobbers: A, B, HL, I.
i8080 PORT: the 8080 has no IM 2.
The porter must emulate this
setup_im2 83AD DI no IRQs while we build the vector table (i8080: DI)
83AE LD HL,$BB00 HL -> vector-table page base $BB00
83B1 LD A,H A = $BB = high byte of the table page
83B2 LD I,A I = $BB: IM2 reads vectors from page I (Z80-only)
83B4 DEC A A = $BA = the byte we flood the table with
83B5 LD B,$00 B = 0 -> DJNZ loops 256 times (Z80 DJNZ: dec B, jump nz)
setup_im2_0 83B7 LD (HL),A table[i] = $BA
83B8 INC HL next table slot
83B9 DJNZ setup_im2_0 fill all 256 bytes of $BB00..$BBFF (i8080: DCR B/JNZ loop)
83BB LD (HL),A +1 byte at $BC00 = $BA: covers the table[$100] off-by-one
83BC LD H,A H = $BA
83BD LD L,A L = $BA -> HL = $BABA (the vector the IRQ will fetch)
83BE LD (HL),$C3 plant C3 = JP opcode at $BABA
83C0 INC L
83C1 LD (HL),$CA low byte of JP target -> $CA
83C3 INC L
83C4 LD (HL),$83 high byte -> $83, so vector = 'JP $83CA' = irq_handler
83C6 IM 2 switch CPU to interrupt mode 2 (Z80-only)
83C8 EI interrupts on
83C9: Routine at 83C9
Used by the routine at irq_handler.
im2_noop_ret 83C9 RET return to game_entry
83CA: IRQ_HANDLER -- IM 2 interrupt service routine (50 Hz vblank).
Used by the routine at im2_dispatch.
Reached via the planted vector at $BABA -> JP here.
It is a deliberate NEAR-NO-OP: 'CALL $83C9' targets the bare RET at the end of setup_im2, so the call does nothing.
The point is to REPLACE the 48K ROM's own interrupt routine (which scans the keyboard and bumps the FRAMES sysvar) with a handler that has no side effects, while still letting interrupts fire.
Verified: no code anywhere
irq_handler 83CA CALL im2_noop_ret CALL $83C9 = the RET in setup_im2: a no-op (neutralized ISR)
83CD EI re-enable interrupts
83CE RETI RETI: return from maskable interrupt (Z80-only
83D0: INIT_TOWER_POS -- reset the tower/scroll position for a new level: tower_height=$04, $60BA/$60BC (current & target column)=8,
Used by the routine at setup_level.
scroll_pos = 8-$28, and clear the high bytes $60BB/$60BD.
Clobbers: A.
init_tower_pos 83D0 LD A,$04
83D2 LD ($60B9),A
83D5 LD A,$08
83D7 LD ($60BA),A

83DF LD ($6030),A

83FB LD ($6020),A
83FE LD ($60C5),A
8401 LD A,$00
8403 LD ($60C0),A
8406 LD A,$80
8408 LD ($60BE),A
840B LD A,$01
840D LD ($60C2),A
8410 RET
8411: ROTATE_TOWER -- rotate the tower by signed B: tower_height = (tower_height + B) AND $7F (128-column wrap), saving the old value in $6033.
Used by the routines at rotate_if_idle, pstate_play, pstate_2, pstate_1 and pstate_4.
Tests the new position for a wall/collision ($9330) on collision (NC) it reverts tower_height.
In: B=delta.
Out: CY=moved.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
rotate_tower 8411 LD B,A
8412 LD A,($60B9)
8415 LD ($6033),A
8418 ADD A,B
8419 AND $7F
841B LD ($60B9),A
841E LD HL,($60BA)
8421 CALL check_rotate_collision
8424 JR NC,rotate_tower_0
8426 LD A,($6033)
8429 LD ($60B9),A
842C SCF
842D RET
rotate_tower_0 842E OR A
842F RET
8430: STEP_SCROLL -- advance the column/scroll position $60BA by signed A (sign-extended via B), folding H into the 4-quadrant range (AND $03) saves the previous position in $6034.

8459 RET

845B RET
step_scroll_2 845C POP HL
845D JP code_8a71_3

8482: PLAYER_TICK -- per-frame player state machine, run every frame from main_tick (both in normal play AND on the game_state==3 level-transition path TASK.md called this 'the level-transition path' -- it is really the general player update, the transition is just one caller).
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Preamble debounces directional input: input_latch ($6020) records whether input_x is active so a press fires once a held input is consumed (input_x cleared).

89C5: CHECK_COLLISION -- test the player against the tower/hazards and apply the result.
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Dispatches on player_state ($60C0):
7 = invulnerable/idle -> no test (RET)
4 = check a height window ($60BF in $0A..$1E)
8 = RET else test flag $60C5.
Calls $895B (carry = hit).
On a hit it patches the value at $8A49 (SELF-MODIFYING: $14/$0F/$20 select the bump/death response used downstream) and branches into the hit handler.
i8080 PORT: the LD ($8A49),A se
check_collision 89C5 LD A,($60C0) A = player_state ($60C0)
89C8 CP $07
89CA RET Z state 7: no collision this frame
89CB CP $04
89CD JR NZ,check_collision_0
89CF LD A,($60BF) A = $60BF (height-in-column)
89D2 CP $0A
89D4 JR C,check_collision_1
89D6 CP $1F
89D8 JR NC,check_collision_1
89DA RET
check_collision_0 89DB CP $08
89DD RET Z
89DE LD A,($60C5)
89E1 OR A
89E2 JR NZ,check_collision_6
check_collision_1 89E4 CALL collision_test test player vs world (carry = hit)
89E7 JR C,check_collision_2 hit -> hit handler
89E9 LD A,($60C0)
89EC CP $06
89EE RET NZ
89EF LD A,($60B9)
89F2 LD HL,($60BA)
89F5 CALL check_rotate_collision
89F8 RET NC
89F9 LD A,$14
89FB LD ($8A49),A patch response code at $8A49 = $14 (SELF-MOD)
89FE JP check_collision_4
check_collision_2 8A01 LD A,(IX+$30) A = colliding object state (+48)
8A04 LD B,$0F
8A06 CP $07
8A08 JR Z,check_collision_3
8A0A LD B,$14
check_collision_3 8A0C LD A,B
8A0D LD ($8A49),A patch response code ($0F enemy / $14 wall)
8A10 LD A,($60C0)
8A13 CP $06
8A15 JR NZ,check_collision_5
check_collision_4 8A17 LD A,($60BA)
8A1A AND $03
8A1C JR Z,check_collision_7
8A1E LD HL,$60C5
8A21 INC (HL)
8A22 RET
check_collision_5 8A23 JP coll_apply
check_collision_6 8A26 LD A,($60C0)
8A29 CP $06
8A2B JR NZ,check_collision_9
8A2D LD A,($60BA)
8A30 AND $03
8A32 RET NZ
check_collision_7 8A33 CALL check_conveyor
8A36 CALL jp_alloc_slot
8A39 JR NC,check_collision_8
8A3B LD (HL),$8D
check_collision_8 8A3D LD A,$FF
8A3F LD ($6038),A
check_collision_9 8A42 XOR A
8A43 LD ($60C5),A
8A46 JP coll_apply

8B50: SPRITE_SETUP -- compute on-screen positions for the 4 actor sprites (objects 0..3) ahead of drawing.
Used by the routine at draw_player.
Derives a buffer base from tower_height into $600A, fills two coordinate tables at $8B30/$8B40 (X and Y per sprite, via $8BEC/$8BFF), then per object copies sprite id (IX+18 -> IX+24) and stores the screen column (IX+30) from the angular position (IX+0).
Self-mod: $8B30/$8B40 are coordinate scratch buffers IN code.
i8080 PORT: IX object walk -> HL
sprite_setup 8B50 LD HL,$60B9 HL -> tower_height
8B53 LD A,$04
8B55 SUB (HL) 4 - height, low 3 bits -> sub-row
8B56 AND $07
8B58 LD L,$00
8B5A SRL A
8B5C RR L
8B5E LD H,A
8B5F LD DE,$6600 DE = $6600 sprite work buffer base
8B62 ADD HL,DE
8B63 LD ($600A),HL store buffer ptr -> $600A
8B66 LD HL,$8B30 fill X-coord table $8B30 (4 sprites, step 8)
8B69 LD A,$04
sprite_setup_0 8B6B PUSH AF
8B6C CALL col_screen_y
8B6F ADD A,$1E
8B71 LD (HL),A
8B72 INC HL
8B73 POP AF
8B74 ADD A,$08
8B76 CP $84
8B78 JR NZ,sprite_setup_0
8B7A LD HL,$8B40 fill Y-coord table $8B40 (4 sprites)
8B7D LD A,$04
sprite_setup_1 8B7F PUSH AF
8B80 CALL col_screen_x
8B83 LD (HL),A
8B84 INC HL
8B85 POP AF
8B86 ADD A,$08
8B88 CP $84
8B8A JR NZ,sprite_setup_1
8B8C LD IX,$605B IX -> object_table
8B90 LD B,$04
sprite_setup_2 8B92 PUSH BC
8B93 LD A,(IX+$12) back up sprite id (IX+18 -> IX+24)
8B96 LD (IX+$18),A
8B99 LD A,(IX+$30)
8B9C CP $00 state 0 (inactive) -> skip
8B9E JR Z,sprite_setup_4
8BA0 CP $07
8BA2 LD A,$40 state 7 -> fixed screen col $40
8BA4 JR Z,sprite_setup_3
8BA6 LD A,(IX+$00)
8BA9 CALL col_screen_x else screen col from angular pos (IX+0)
sprite_setup_3 8BAC LD (IX+$1E),A store sprite screen column (IX+30)
8BAF LD L,(IX+$06)
8BB2 LD H,(IX+$0C)
8BB5 LD DE,($6057)
8BB9 OR A
8BBA PUSH HL
8BBB SBC HL,DE
8BBD POP HL
8BBE JR C,sprite_setup_6
8BC0 LD DE,($6059)
8BC4 OR A
8BC5 SBC HL,DE
8BC7 JR NC,sprite_setup_4
8BC9 LD A,(IX+$30)
8BCC CP $07
8BCE JR Z,sprite_setup_5
8BD0 CALL calc_sprite_shift
8BD3 JR NC,sprite_setup_5
sprite_setup_4 8BD5 LD (IX+$18),$FF
sprite_setup_5 8BD9 POP BC
8BDA INC IX
8BDC DJNZ sprite_setup_2
8BDE RET
sprite_setup_6 8BDF LD A,(IX+$30)
8BE2 CP $07
8BE4 JR Z,sprite_setup_4
8BE6 LD (IX+$30),$00
8BEA JR sprite_setup_4
8BEC: COL_SCREEN_Y -- map an actor's angular column to its screen Y / vertical-projection value.
Used by the routines at sprite_setup and calc_sprite_shift.
In: A = angular position (0..127).
Offsets by the tower rotation: rel = (A + $20 - tower_height) & $7F, then returns col_y_table[$6380 + rel].
Out: A = projected value.
Preserves HL (PUSH/POP).
i8080 PORT: the AND $7F is the around-the-tower wrap (128 cols) $6380 is a precomputed projection table -- port it verbatim and index with HL + rel.
col_screen_y 8BEC PUSH HL
8BED ADD A,$20 bias by +$20 (quarter turn)
8BEF LD HL,$60B9
8BF2 SUB (HL) subtract current tower rotation
8BF3 AND $7F wrap to 0..127 (around the tower)
8BF5 LD E,A
8BF6 LD D,$00
8BF8 LD HL,$6380 HL -> col_y_table ($6380)
8BFB ADD HL,DE
8BFC LD A,(HL) A = table[rel] = projected Y/value
8BFD POP HL
8BFE RET
8BFF: COL_SCREEN_X -- map an actor's angular column to a screen X column, folding the cylinder so only the front face shows.
Used by the routine at sprite_setup.
In: A = angular position (0..127).
rel = (A + $20 - tower_height) & $7F, then fold: rel < $20 -> rel + $20 (left quadrant) $20..$5F -> $60 - rel (front, mirrored) rel >= $60 -> rel - $60 (right quadrant) Out: A = screen column.
Preserves HL.
i8080 PORT: pure arithmetic fold of a 7-bit ang
col_screen_x 8BFF PUSH HL
8C00 ADD A,$20 bias +$20
8C02 LD HL,$60B9
8C05 SUB (HL) subtract tower rotation
8C06 AND $7F wrap to 0..127
8C08 CP $20 left quadrant?
8C0A JR NC,col_screen_x_0
8C0C ADD A,$20 -> rel + $20
8C0E JR col_screen_x_2
col_screen_x_0 8C10 CP $60 back face?
8C12 JR C,col_screen_x_1
8C14 SUB $60 -> rel - $60 (right quadrant)
8C16 JR col_screen_x_2
col_screen_x_1 8C18 LD H,A
8C19 LD A,$60
8C1B SUB H front: mirror $60 - rel
col_screen_x_2 8C1C POP HL
8C1D RET
8C1E: SPRITE_SHIFT_MASKS -- 1bpp AND/shift mask data for drawing the tower-column sprites at sub-byte X offsets (patterns 3F/0F/03/00 FF...).
Base referenced from $8D35/$8F5E.
Interleaved with a few SELF-MODIFIED 1-byte slots ($8C76/$8C77/$8C78/$8C81/$8C82/$8C83/ $8C85) that calc_sprite_shift patches with computed shift counts.
Spans $8C1E..$8CC1 code resumes at calc_sprite_shift.
i8080 PORT: keep as a RAM mask table the self-mod slots become normal
sprite_shift_masks 8C1E DEFB $FF,$FF,$FC,$FF,$FF,$F0,$FF,$FF
8C26 DEFB $C0,$FF,$FF,$00,$FF,$FC,$00,$FF
8C2E DEFB $F0,$00,$FF,$C0,$00,$FF,$00,$00
8C36 DEFB $FC,$00,$00,$F0,$00,$00,$C0,$00
8C3E DEFB $00,$3F,$FF,$FF,$0F,$FF,$FF,$03
8C46 DEFB $FF,$FF,$00,$FF,$FF,$00,$3F,$FF
8C4E DEFB $00,$0F,$FF,$00,$03,$FF,$00,$00
8C56 DEFB $FF,$00,$00,$3F,$00,$00,$0F,$00
8C5E DEFB $00,$03,$C0,$03,$FF,$F0,$00,$FF
8C66 DEFB $FC,$00,$3F,$FF,$00,$0F,$FF,$C0
8C6E DEFB $03,$C0,$00,$03,$00,$00,$00,$00
8C76 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$08,$02,$06,$04
8C7E DEFB $04,$06,$02,$00,$00,$00,$00,$FF
8C86 DEFB $FF,$FF,$03,$03,$03,$07,$07,$07
8C8E DEFB $07,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8C96 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8C9E DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8CA6 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8CAE DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8CB6 DEFB $00,$00,$12,$12,$12,$0E,$0E,$0E
8CBE DEFB $0E,$FF,$FF,$FF
8CC2: CALC_SPRITE_SHIFT -- for the actor at IX, compute its screen row (via col_screen_y) and the horizontal sub-byte shift, and patch the shift counts into the self-mod slots above ($8C76/77/ 81).
Used by the routine at sprite_setup.
Reads the column buffer ($600A) to skip empty/edge columns ($0F/$0E sentinels).
Called from sprite_setup ($8BD0).
i8080 PORT: replace self-mod patching with RAM shift vars the AND $7F / SRL x3 is the around-tower wrap + /8 byte-column calc.
calc_sprite_shift 8CC2 LD A,(IX+$00) A = actor angular pos (IX+0)
8CC5 CALL col_screen_y -> screen row / projected Y
8CC8 ADD A,$1E
8CCA ADD A,$02
8CCC AND $FC
8CCE LD ($8C81),A patch self-mod slot $8C81 (row)
8CD1 LD HL,($600A) HL -> column buffer ($600A)
8CD4 ADD HL,DE
8CD5 LD A,(HL)
8CD6 CP $0F $0F = empty column -> skip (RET)
8CD8 RET Z
8CD9 CP $0E
8CDB CCF
8CDC RET Z
8CDD LD B,A
8CDE LD A,($60B9) A = tower_height (rotation)
8CE1 SUB $1D
8CE3 AND $7F
8CE5 SRL A /8 -> byte-column shift
8CE7 SRL A
8CE9 SRL A
8CEB LD C,A
8CEC ADD A,B
8CED AND $0F
8CEF LD ($8C77),A patch shift count slot $8C77
8CF2 LD B,A
8CF3 SRL A
8CF5 SRL A
8CF7 SRL A
8CF9 SRL A
8CFB ADD A,C
8CFC AND $0F
8CFE LD ($8C76),A patch shift count slot $8C76
8D01 LD HL,$FFFF
8D04 LD ($600C),HL
8D07 LD ($600E),HL
8D0A LD ($6010),HL
8D0D LD ($6012),HL
8D10 LD ($6013),HL
8D13 XOR A
8D14 LD ($8C78),A
8D17 LD A,(IX+$00)
8D1A CALL col_screen_y
8D1D BIT 6,E
8D1F LD ($8C83),A
8D22 JR Z,calc_sprite_shift_0
8D24 ADD A,$1E
8D26 LD E,A
8D27 LD D,$00
8D29 LD HL,$8C85
8D2C ADD HL,DE
8D2D LD A,(HL)
8D2E CP $FF
8D30 JR Z,calc_sprite_shift_0

8E23 LD B,$00
8E25: EXPAND_SPRITE_A -- unrolled copy of a sprite/mask definition from (HL) to (DE), terminated by $FF.
Used by the routine at calc_sprite_shift.
The alternating OR $00 / AND $00 immediates are SELF-MODIFIED (by calc_sprite_shift) to apply the per-column shift mask.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
i8080: self-modifying immediates -- a port may instead pass the mask in a register and AND/OR it.
expand_sprite_a 8E25 LD A,(HL)
8E26 OR $00
8E28 LD (DE),A
8E29 INC HL
8E2A INC DE
8E2B LD A,(HL)
8E2C AND $00
8E2E LD (DE),A
8E2F INC HL
8E30 INC DE
8E31 LD A,(HL)
8E32 OR $00
8E34 LD (DE),A
8E35 INC HL
8E36 INC DE
8E37 LD A,(HL)
8E38 AND $00
8E3A LD (DE),A
8E3B INC HL
8E3C INC DE
8E3D LD A,$FF
8E3F LD (DE),A
8E40 INC DE
8E41 INC A
8E42 LD (DE),A
8E43 INC DE
8E44 DJNZ expand_sprite_a
8E46 OR A
8E47 RET
This entry point is used by the routine at calc_sprite_shift.
expand_sprite_a_0 8E48 LD A,($8C82)
8E4B ADD A,A
8E4C ADD A,$79
8E4E LD C,A
8E4F LD A,$8C
8E51 ADC A,$00
8E53 LD B,A
8E54 LD A,(BC)
8E55 LD ($8E82),A
8E58 INC BC
8E59 LD A,(BC)
8E5A LD ($8EC8),A
8E5D LD A,($8C82)
8E60 OR A
8E61 JR Z,expand_sprite_a_1

8E6D LD A,($600D)
8E70 LD ($8EDD),A

8E77 LD A,($600E)
8E7A LD ($8EE9),A
8E7D CPL
8E7E LD ($8EE3),A
8E81 LD B,$00
8E83 CALL expand_sprite_b
expand_sprite_a_1 8E86 LD A,($600F)

8ECA OR $00
8ECC LD (DE),A

8ED0 AND $00
8ED2 LD (DE),A

8EED DJNZ expand_sprite_b

8EF1: CALC_SPRITE_COL -- resolve a sprite column: index table $8B30 by A, compare the result against the actor's clip field (IX+30), and patch the shift slot $8C81.
Used by the routine at calc_sprite_shift.
RET early when within bounds.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
calc_sprite_col 8EF1 LD E,A
8EF2 ADD A,$F0
8EF4 LD H,A
8EF5 LD D,$00
8EF7 PUSH HL
8EF8 LD HL,$8B30
8EFB ADD HL,DE
8EFC LD A,(HL)
8EFD LD DE,$0010
8F00 ADD HL,DE
8F01 LD E,A
8F02 LD A,(HL)
8F03 CP (IX+$1E)
8F06 JR NC,calc_sprite_col_0
8F08 POP HL
8F09 RET
calc_sprite_col_0 8F0A LD A,E
8F0B LD HL,$8C81
8F0E SUB (HL)
8F0F ADD A,$0B
8F11 POP HL
8F12 RET M
8F13 CP $16
8F15 RET NC
8F16 LD E,A
8F17 PUSH DE
8F18 LD BC,$6012
8F1B CALL draw_tower_cell
8F1E INC L
8F1F POP DE
8F20 PUSH DE
8F21 LD BC,$600F
8F24 CALL draw_tower_cell
8F27 INC L
8F28 POP DE
8F29 LD A,($8C82)
8F2C OR A
8F2D RET Z
8F2E LD BC,$600C
8F31: DRAW_TOWER_CELL -- decode one tower cell descriptor byte at (HL): the sign bit and low 2/4 bits select which feature renderer (door / block / gap, pointers $8F8B etc.) to run.
Used by the routine at calc_sprite_col.
Clobbers: A,HL,...
draw_tower_cell 8F31 PUSH HL
8F32 LD A,(HL)
8F33 OR A
8F34 JP P,draw_tower_cell_3
8F37 LD H,A
8F38 AND $03
8F3A CP $03
8F3C JR Z,draw_tower_cell_3
8F3E SRL A
8F40 JR NC,draw_tower_cell_1
8F42 LD A,H
8F43 AND $0C
8F45 JR Z,draw_tower_cell_0
8F47 LD HL,$8F8B
8F4A JR draw_tower_cell_2
draw_tower_cell_0 8F4C LD HL,$8F75
8F4F JR draw_tower_cell_2
draw_tower_cell_1 8F51 LD HL,$8FA1
draw_tower_cell_2 8F54 ADD HL,DE
8F55 LD A,(HL)
8F56 OR A
8F57 JP M,draw_tower_cell_3
8F5A LD E,A
8F5B ADD A,A
8F5C ADD A,E
8F5D LD E,A
8F5E LD HL,$8C1E
8F61 ADD HL,DE
8F62 LD A,(BC)
8F63 AND (HL)
8F64 LD (BC),A
8F65 INC BC
8F66 INC HL
8F67 LD A,(BC)
8F68 AND (HL)
8F69 LD (BC),A
8F6A INC BC
8F6B INC HL
8F6C LD A,(BC)
8F6D AND (HL)
8F6E LD (BC),A
8F6F LD HL,$8C78
8F72 INC (HL)
draw_tower_cell_3 8F73 POP HL
8F74 RET
8F75: Data block at 8F75
8F75 DEFB $0B,$0C,$0D,$0E,$0F,$10,$11,$12
8F7D DEFB $13,$14,$15,$1C,$0A,$09,$08,$07
8F85 DEFB $06,$05,$04,$03,$02,$01,$FF,$0B
8F8D DEFB $0C,$0D,$0E,$0F,$10,$11,$12,$13
8F95 DEFB $14,$1B,$09,$08,$07,$06,$05,$04

8FA5 DEFB $0C,$0D,$0E,$0F,$10,$16,$17,$18
8FAD DEFB $19,$1A,$05,$04,$03,$02,$01,$00
8FB5 DEFB $FF,$FF
8FB7: CLEAR_ACTOR_STATES -- zero the state byte (+48) of all four object_table actors, deactivating every enemy.
Used by the routine at setup_level.
Clobbers: A,B,IX.
clear_actor_states 8FB7 LD IX,$605B
8FBB LD A,$00
8FBD LD B,$04
clear_actor_states_0 8FBF LD (IX+$30),A
8FC2 INC IX
8FC4 DJNZ clear_actor_states_0
8FC6 RET
8FC7: DRAW_PLAYER -- compute the player sprite's on-screen address from the vertical scroll ($6030) and draw it.
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Handles the tower wrap: the playfield scrolls vertically mod the tower height, so the screen row is ($6030 +/- offset) kept in range.
Stores the two derived screen pointers ($6057, $6059) then calls the blit helpers ($8B50, $9035).
In: $6030 = scroll position.
Out: player drawn.
Clobbers A,HL.
i8080 PORT: the SBC/AND $03 wrap math maps the s
draw_player 8FC7 LD HL,($6030) HL = scroll position ($6030)
8FCA LD A,L
8FCB SUB $08 - 8 (sprite top offset)
8FCD LD L,A
8FCE LD A,H
8FCF SBC A,$00
8FD1 AND $03 keep within the 3 screen-thirds (mod via AND 3)
8FD3 LD H,A
8FD4 CP $03 past the wrap point ($3D0)?
8FD6 JR NZ,draw_player_0
8FD8 LD A,L
8FD9 CP $D0
8FDB JR C,draw_player_0
8FDD LD HL,$0000 -> clamp to top (0)
draw_player_0 8FE0 LD ($6057),HL store upper screen pointer ($6057)
8FE3 LD HL,($6030)
8FE6 LD A,L
8FE7 ADD A,$4D + $4D (sprite body offset)
8FE9 LD L,A
8FEA LD A,H
8FEB ADC A,$00
8FED AND $03
8FEF LD H,A
8FF0 LD ($6059),HL store lower screen pointer ($6059)
8FF3 CALL sprite_setup blit sprite (mask + data)
8FF6 CALL sprite_depth_sort draw attached widgets
8FF9 RET
8FFA: DRAW_ACTORS_SORTED -- draw the 4 actors back-to-front: for depth key C=3..0, find the object whose field (+36) matches and draw it via draw_actor.
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Clobbers: A,BC,IX.
draw_actors_sorted 8FFA LD C,$03
draw_actors_sorted_0 8FFC LD IX,$605B
9000 LD B,$04
9002 LD A,C
draw_actors_sorted_1 9003 CP (IX+$24)
9006 JR Z,draw_actors_sorted_3
9008 INC IX
900A DJNZ draw_actors_sorted_1
draw_actors_sorted_2 900C DEC C
900D JP P,draw_actors_sorted_0
9010 JR draw_actors_sorted_4
draw_actors_sorted_3 9012 PUSH BC
9013 CALL jp_draw_actor
9016 POP BC
9017 JR draw_actors_sorted_2
draw_actors_sorted_4 9019 LD IX,$605F
901D LD A,(IX+$2A)
9020 CP $FF
9022 RET Z
9023 LD A,(IX+$12)
9026 ADD A,$4A
9028 LD (IX+$18),A
902B CALL jp_draw_actor
902E LD A,($60A6)
9031 LD ($60A5),A
9034 RET
9035: SPRITE_DEPTH_SORT -- decide the back-to-front draw order of the 4 actor sprites so nearer ones overdraw farther ones around the tower.
Used by the routine at draw_player.
Does all 6 pairwise comparisons of the sprites' screen columns (IX+30..IX+33), packing the results into 6 bits of B, XORs $3F, and uses that as an index into data_908e -- a precomputed table mapping each comparison outcome to a draw permutation.
The 4 order indices are unpacked (2 bits each) into IX+36..IX+39.
TRI
sprite_depth_sort 9035 LD IX,$605B IX -> object_table
9039 LD B,$00
903B LD A,(IX+$1E) compare sprite0 col vs sprite1..3
903E CP (IX+$1F)
9041 RL B shift compare result into key (RL B)
9043 CP (IX+$20)
9046 RL B
9048 CP (IX+$21)
904B RL B
904D LD A,(IX+$1F)
9050 CP (IX+$20)
9053 RL B
9055 CP (IX+$21)
9058 RL B
905A LD A,(IX+$20)
905D CP (IX+$21)
9060 RL B
9062 LD A,B A = packed 6-bit comparison key
9063 XOR $3F invert (XOR $3F) to index orientation
9065 LD C,A
9066 LD B,$00
9068 LD HL,$908E HL -> data_908e[key]
906B ADD HL,BC
906C LD B,(HL) B = packed draw order (4 x 2-bit indices)
906D LD A,B
906E AND $03
9070 LD (IX+$27),A unpack order[0] -> IX+39
9073 LD A,B
9074 RRA
9075 RRA
9076 LD B,A
9077 AND $03
9079 LD (IX+$26),A unpack order[1] -> IX+38
907C LD A,B
907D RRA
907E RRA
907F LD B,A
9080 AND $03
9082 LD (IX+$25),A unpack order[2] -> IX+37
9085 LD A,B
9086 RRA
9087 RRA
9088 AND $03
908A LD (IX+$24),A unpack order[3] -> IX+36
908D RET return: draw order in IX+36..+39
908E: DATA_908E -- 64-entry packed draw-order permutation table for sprite_depth_sort.
Index = 6-bit pairwise-comparison key of the 4 sprites' columns (XOR $3F).
Each byte packs 4 two-bit object
indices = the back-to-front draw order.
Bounded by update_enemies.
data_908e 908E DEFB $E4,$E1,$00,$D2,$D8,$00,$C9,$C6
9096 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$93,$00,$00,$00,$87
909E DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$9C,$00,$8D,$00
90A6 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$4E,$4B
90AE DEFB $B4,$B1,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
90B6 DEFB $00,$72,$00,$63,$00,$00,$00,$00
90BE DEFB $78,$00,$00,$00,$6C,$00,$00,$00
90C6 DEFB $39,$36,$00,$27,$2D,$00,$1E,$1B
90CE DEFB $02,$02,$01,$01,$00,$00,$FF,$FF
90D6 DEFB $FE,$FE,$FC,$00
90DA: UPDATE_ENEMIES -- step the 4 enemy actors (objects 0..3 of object_table).
Used by the routine at main_tick.
For each: dispatch on its state (+48):
0 = inactive (skip), 7 = patrol/home toward player_col,
4/6 = -> $9158, 5 = phase countdown (+42).
State 7 homing: moves angular pos (+0) by speed (+54), then compares against player_col and the tower quadrant marks $3C/$78 to pick a chase direction (+2 / -2), or deactivates.
In: nothing.
Loops B=4 objects.
Clobbers most regs + IX.
update_enemies 90DA LD IX,$605B IX -> object_table (enemy 0)
90DE LD B,$04 B = 4 enemies
update_enemies_0 90E0 PUSH BC
90E1 LD A,(IX+$30) A = this enemy's state (+48)
90E4 CP $00
90E6 JP Z,update_enemies_33 0 = inactive -> next enemy
90E9 CP $07
90EB JR NZ,update_enemies_6 state 7 = active/homing?
90ED LD (IX+$12),$4E set sprite id (+18) = $4E
90F1 LD A,(IX+$00)
90F4 ADD A,(IX+$36) angular pos (+0) += speed (+54)
90F7 LD (IX+$00),A
90FA LD HL,$60A4 HL -> player_col
90FD BIT 7,(HL) bit7 = which side of the tower the player is on
90FF JR NZ,update_enemies_2
9101 CP $3C at quadrant mark $3C?
9103 JR Z,update_enemies_1
9105 CP $78
9107 JR NZ,update_enemies_5
9109 JR update_enemies_4
update_enemies_1 910B LD (IX+$36),$02 chase: speed = +2 (toward player)
910F JR update_enemies_5
update_enemies_2 9111 CP $3C
9113 JR Z,update_enemies_3
9115 OR A
9116 JR NZ,update_enemies_5
9118 JR update_enemies_4
update_enemies_3 911A LD (IX+$36),$FE chase: speed = -2 ($FE)
911E JR update_enemies_5
update_enemies_4 9120 LD (IX+$30),$00 off-screen/limit -> deactivate (state 0)
9124 LD A,$7D flag $60A3 = $7D (respawn/notify)
9126 LD ($60A3),A
update_enemies_5 9129 JP update_enemies_33
update_enemies_6 912C CP $04 state 4?
912E JP Z,update_enemies_9
9131 CP $06
9133 JP Z,update_enemies_9
9136 CP $05 state 5 = phase countdown
9138 JP NZ,update_enemies_8
913B DEC (IX+$2A) decrement phase (+42)
913E BIT 7,(IX+$2A)
9142 JR Z,update_enemies_7
9144 LD (IX+$30),$02
update_enemies_7 9148 JP update_enemies_33
update_enemies_8 914B CALL check_actor_collision_a
914E JR NC,update_enemies_9
9150 LD (IX+$30),$04
9154 LD (IX+$2A),$00
update_enemies_9 9158 LD A,(IX+$30)
915B CP $04
915D JR Z,update_enemies_11
915F CP $06
9161 JR NZ,update_enemies_14
9163 LD A,(IX+$2A)
9166 CP $06
9168 JR Z,update_enemies_10
916A NEG
916C ADD A,$05
916E JR update_enemies_12
update_enemies_10 9170 LD A,(IX+$42)
9173 LD (IX+$30),A
9176 LD A,(IX+$3C)
9179 LD (IX+$2A),A
917C LD (IX+$12),$52
9180 JP update_enemies_33
update_enemies_11 9183 LD A,(IX+$2A)
9186 CP $06
9188 JR NZ,update_enemies_12
918A LD (IX+$30),$00
918E JP update_enemies_33
update_enemies_12 9191 CP $03
9193 JR C,update_enemies_13

update_enemies_13 9197 ADD A,A

9330: CHECK_ROTATE_COLLISION -- player-move collision probe.
Used by the routines at rotate_tower, step_scroll, check_collision and code_8a71.
Loads the self-mod probe params (column $9329=A, direction step $932A=$FD, span $932B/$932C, plus $9432/$9437) then runs the shared cell probe at $9395.
In: A=column.
Out: CY/flags from the probe.
check_rotate_collision 9330 LD ($9329),A
9333 LD A,$FD
9335 LD ($932A),A
9338 LD A,$02
933A LD ($932B),A
933D XOR A
933E LD ($932C),A
9341 LD ($9432),A
9344 LD A,$09
9346 LD ($9437),A
9349 JP run_cell_probe

move_curve 96DD DEFB $00,$01,$00,$01,$00,$01,$00,$00

96E9: UPDATE_ACTOR_POS -- advance one actor's position around/along the tower for this frame.
Used by the routine at main_tick.
IX = actor_obj ($605F).
Fields used: (IX+0) angular column around the tower (accumulated mod 128) (IX+6) vertical position, low (sub-pixel) (IX+12) vertical position, high (0..3) (IX+18) per-frame scratch, cleared on entry (IX+42) animation/move phase 0..$0C ($FF = inactive/done) (IX+48) actor state (2,4,5,8,9 -- see transitions below) (IX+54)
update_actor_pos 96E9 LD IX,$605F IX -> actor struct base ($605F)
96ED LD (IX+$12),$00 clear per-frame scratch field (+18)
96F1 LD A,(IX+$2A) A = phase (+42)
96F4 CP $FF $FF = inactive -> nothing to do
96F6 RET Z
96F7 CP $0C phase $0C = end of move -> mark done ($FF)
96F9 JR NZ,update_actor_pos_1
update_actor_pos_0 96FB LD (IX+$2A),$FF
96FF RET
update_actor_pos_1 9700 LD E,A E = phase (table index)
9701 INC (IX+$2A) advance phase
9704 LD A,(IX+$36) A = speed(+54) * 2
9707 ADD A,A
9708 ADD A,(IX+$00) + angular pos (+0)
970B AND $7F wrap to 0..127 (around the tower)
970D LD (IX+$00),A store new angular column (+0)
9710 LD D,$00
9712 LD HL,$96DD HL -> move_curve[phase]
9715 ADD HL,DE
9716 LD A,(IX+$06) add curve delta to vertical-low (+6)
9719 ADD A,(HL)
971A LD (IX+$06),A
971D LD A,(IX+$0C) carry into vertical-high (+12), keep 0..3
9720 ADC A,$00
9722 AND $03
9724 LD (IX+$0C),A
9727 CALL check_actor_collision_b collision test (carry set = blocked)
972A JR C,update_actor_pos_0 blocked -> mark move done
972C CALL check_collisions landing/edge test (NC = no event)
972F RET NC
9730 PUSH IX
9732 PUSH HL
9733 POP IX
9735 LD A,(IX+$30) branch on actor state (+48)
9738 CP $05
973A JR Z,update_actor_pos_3
973C CP $08
973E JR Z,update_actor_pos_3
9740 CP $09
9742 JR Z,update_actor_pos_3
9744 CP $02
9746 JR NZ,update_actor_pos_2
9748 LD (IX+$2A),$4B state 2: arm phase=$4B, state=5
974C LD (IX+$30),$05
9750 JR update_actor_pos_3
update_actor_pos_2 9752 LD (IX+$30),$04 default: state=4, phase=0
9756 LD (IX+$2A),$00
975A LD HL,$0100
975D CALL jp_add_score
9760 LD A,$05
9762 CALL jp_field_604f_add
9765 LD A,$09
9767 CALL play_sound
update_actor_pos_3 976A POP IX
976C JP update_actor_pos_0

99A1: DRAW_INFO_SCREEN_A -- render an animated info/message screen: tick the title animation, (re)start jingle 2, and print the screen's text lines (strings at $981E..) via print_string_at, running the music sequencer.

menu_page 9B87 DEFB $00,$BC,$01
9B8A: TITLE_MENU -- title screen, option menu and attract/demo loop.
Used by the routine at level_retry.
(TASK.md guessed 'init level data' here -- WRONG verified to be the front-end.) Blits the 24-row NEBULUS logo bitmap from rt_be00 ($BE00) into the screen, paints attribute rows, and prints the option lines (1/2 PLAYER etc.) via draw_menu_text.
Runs proc_table as an attract demo until input, then polls the keyboard ($FE) and Kempston ($1F) for select/start.

A1BD: MUSIC_TICK -- per-frame music sequencer step (DI around it).
Used by the routines at code_9963, draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen and 9BA5.
When the note timer ($A195) elapses, fetch the next event from the stream ($A190), update the current note ($A192), and play it.
i8080: speaker via OUT ($FE) bit4 (ULA).
The Specialist has a 1-bit beeper too -- retarget the port and retune the delay loops.

A1BE LD A,($A195)
A1C1 OR A

A3DE: PLAY_SOUND -- trigger sound effect A (id < $0D).
Used by the routines at main_tick, pstate_play, pstate_2, code_8656, pstate_1, 8724, pstate_4, 8831, pstate_6, coll_apply, code_8a71, update_enemies, check_actor_collision_b, consume_cell, update_actor_pos, attract_step and A113.
Saves all regs, looks up the effect via lookup_sound, and (subject to a priority check against the currently playing effect at $A368/$A369) latches its data pointer at $A36A for sound_tick to play out.
In: A=effect id.
Preserves all registers.
Most-called audio API.

A3DF PUSH BC

A41C: SOUND_TICK -- per-frame SFX engine step.
Used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
Gated by the sound-enable flag ($6056) and the active marker ($A368).
Reads the next byte of the current effect (pointer $A36A), interpreting commands (>=$80) vs tone/length data, and emits to the speaker.
i8080: speaker via OUT ($FE) bit4 (ULA).
The Specialist has a 1-bit beeper too -- retarget the port and retune the delay loops.

A526 LD ($A518),A
A529 CALL sfx_tone
A52C POP AF
A52D RET
A52E: Routine at A52E
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A52E PUSH AF
A52F CALL jp_random
A532 AND $03
A534 CALL sfx_tone
A537 POP AF
A538 RET
A539: SFX_TONE -- emit a square-wave tone: pitch index A selects a half-period from table $A560 toggles the speaker for a fixed duration ($0148 outer).
Used by the routines at sound_tick, A4F2, sfx_random_tone and A52E.
In: A=pitch.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL,AF'.
i8080: speaker via OUT ($FE) bit4 (ULA).
The Specialist has a 1-bit beeper too -- retarget the port and retune the delay loops.
sfx_tone A539 LD L,A
A53A LD H,$00
A53C LD DE,$A560
A53F ADD HL,DE
A540 LD DE,$0148
A543 LD C,(HL)
A544 LD A,$10
A546 EX AF,AF'
A547 LD B,$10
A549 LD L,C
sfx_tone_0 A54A DEC L
A54B JR NZ,sfx_tone_2
A54D EX AF,AF'
A54E XOR B
A54F OUT ($FE),A
A551 EX AF,AF'
A552 LD L,C
sfx_tone_1 A553 DEC DE
A554 LD A,D
A555 OR E
A556 JR NZ,sfx_tone_0
A558 RET
sfx_tone_2 A559 OR $00
A55B BIT 0,A
A55D JP sfx_tone_1
A560 DEFM "fa[VQMHD"

A568 DEFB $40
A569 DEFM "=9630-+(&$\" "

A700: PROC_TABLE -- jump/trampoline dispatch table, $A700..$A79E (53 entries, each 'JP nnnn', 3 bytes).
Used by the routines at poll_controls, main_tick, 9BA5, wait_frames and 9D80.
The game CALLs these fixed slots instead of the moving routine bodies a CALL <slot> costs one extra JP.
Targets land in $A79F..$BA67.
i8080 PORT: just a vector table -- keep verbatim (8080 has JMP) or inline each trampoline away.
proc_table A700 JP read_input

A79F: READ_INPUT -- proc_table[0]: sample controls into the signed input_x/input_y/input_fire vars.
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Only active when game_mode ($6017) == 4 (in play) otherwise returns.
Reads the Kempston joystick and converts each axis to -1/0/+1 via bit tests + SBC/NEG.
i8080 PORT: input comes from IN A,($1F) (Kempston).
The Specialist has no Kempston port -- replace read_joystick with the target's keyboard/joystick scan, keeping the -1/0/+1 result.
read_input A79F LD A,($6017) A = game_mode ($6017)
A7A2 CP $04
A7A4 JR NZ,scan_control_keys only read controls while in play (mode 4)
A7A6 CALL read_joystick A = raw joystick bits
A7A9 LD B,A
A7AA AND $01
A7AC SRL B
A7AE SRL B
A7B0 SBC A,$00
A7B2 LD ($601D),A input_y = -1/0/+1
A7B5 LD A,B
A7B6 AND $01
A7B8 SRL B
A7BA SRL B
A7BC SBC A,$00
A7BE NEG
A7C0 LD ($601E),A input_fire = -1/0/+1 (negated)
A7C3 LD A,B
A7C4 LD ($601C),A input_x = -1/0/+1
A7C7 RET
A7C8: Routine at A7C8
Used by the routine at read_input.
read_joystick A7C8 IN A,($1F) read Kempston joystick port $1F (Z80 IN
A7CA AND $1F
A7CC CP $1F all 5 bits = nothing pressed
A7CE RET NZ
A7CF XOR A
A7D0 RET
A7D1: Data block at A7D1
A7D1 DEFB $1D,$15,$19,$22,$0F,$12,$1A,$1E
A7D9 DEFB $25,$08,$24,$1C,$14,$0C,$04,$04
A7E1 DEFB $13,$03,$0B,$23,$D1,$A7,$D6,$A7
A7E9 DEFB $DB,$A7,$E0,$A7
A7ED: SCAN_CONTROL_KEYS -- read one 5-key control set into the flag array at $6018.
Used by the routine at read_input.
A selects the set: index a word-pointer table at $A7E5 (A*2) to a 5-byte key-id list, then test_key each one, storing 0/1 into $6018..$601C.
Used to read the redefinable keys.
In: A=set index.
Out: $6018[0..4] flags.
Clobbers: A,B,DE,HL.
scan_control_keys A7ED ADD A,A
A7EE LD L,A
A7EF LD H,$00
A7F1 LD DE,$A7E5
A7F4 ADD HL,DE
A7F5 LD E,(HL)
A7F6 INC HL
A7F7 LD D,(HL)
A7F8 LD HL,$6018
A7FB LD B,$05
scan_control_keys_0 A7FD LD A,(DE)
A7FE CALL test_key
A801 LD (HL),$00
A803 JR Z,scan_control_keys_1
A805 INC (HL)
scan_control_keys_1 A806 INC HL
A807 INC DE
A808 DJNZ scan_control_keys_0
A80A LD HL,$6018
A80D LD A,(HL)
A80E INC HL
A80F SUB (HL)
A810 INC HL
A811 NEG
A813 LD ($601D),A
A816 LD A,(HL)
A817 INC HL
A818 SUB (HL)
A819 NEG
A81B LD ($601E),A
A81E RET
A81F: TEST_KEY -- is a specific key down?
Used by the routines at jp_test_key and scan_control_keys.
(proc_table slot $A706.) In: A = key id, encoded (row<<3 | column) into the ZX keyboard matrix.
Out: Z set (A=0) if the key is pressed.
Builds the half-row select on the address bus (A=$FE rotated <row> times so one of A8..A15 is low) and reads ULA port $FE rotates out <column> bits and tests the active-low key bit.
i8080 PORT: this is the ZX keyboard hardware scan (IN $FE with address-line row select).
test_key A81F PUSH BC
A820 LD C,A C = key id
A821 AND $07 low 3 bits = column -> bit count
A823 INC A
A824 LD B,A
A825 SRL C C >>= 3 -> row index
A827 SRL C
A829 SRL C
A82B LD A,$05
A82D SUB C 5 - row -> number of rotates for the row select
A82E LD C,A
A82F LD A,$FE A = $FE: one address line low selects a keyboard half-row
test_key_0 A831 RRCA rotate the low bit into the row position (DJNZ)
A832 DJNZ test_key_0
A834 IN A,($FE) read ULA keyboard port $FE (Z80 IN
test_key_1 A836 RRA shift out columns
A837 DEC C
A838 JR NZ,test_key_1
A83A LD A,C
A83B JR C,test_key_2 carry set = not pressed -> A=1
A83D INC A
test_key_2 A83E POP BC
A83F RET
A840: POLL_ANY_KEY -- return NZ if ANY input is active: scans the five keyboard half-rows via IN ($FE) (CPL+AND $1F), and if none, tests the Kempston fire bit ($10).
Out: A/flags NZ = pressed.
i8080: replace IN ($FE)/Kempston with the target's key/fire scan.
poll_any_key A840 DEFB $AF,$DB,$FE,$2F,$E6,$1F,$C0,$CD
A848 DEFB $C8,$A7,$E6,$10,$C9,$CD,$40,$A8
A850 DEFB $20,$FB,$C9
A853: TEST_BREAK_KEY -- test the BREAK combination (CAPS SHIFT + SPACE) via two IN ($FE) row reads.
Used by the routine at jp_test_break.
Returns with CARRY CLEAR only when both are held (used to abort).
Clobbers: A.
test_break_key A853 LD A,$7F
A855 IN A,($FE)
A857 RRA
A858 RET C
A859 LD A,$FE
A85B IN A,($FE)
A85D RRA
A85E RET
A85F: DELAY_LOOP -- busy-wait: B outer iterations of a ~$0086 inner countdown.
In: B=outer count.
Clobbers: A,B,HL (HL preserved).
i8080: a pure timing loop -- retune the constant for 2MHz 8080.
delay_loop A85F DEFB $E5,$21,$86,$00,$2B,$7C,$B5,$20
A867 DEFB $FB,$10,$F6,$E1,$C9
A86C: NEXT_RANDOM -- pseudo-random generator.
Used by the routine at jp_random.
Reads the 16-bit seed ($6002), applies a multiply/shift + add $0029 LCG step, writes the new seed back, and returns A = high byte.
Out: A=random byte, ($6002) advanced.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
next_random A86C PUSH DE
A86D PUSH HL
A86E LD DE,($6002)
A872 LD H,E
A873 LD L,$00
A875 ADD HL,DE
A876 EX DE,HL
A877 ADD HL,HL
A878 ADD HL,HL
A879 ADD HL,HL
A87A EX DE,HL
A87B OR A
A87C SBC HL,DE
A87E LD DE,$0029
A881 ADD HL,DE
A882 LD ($6002),HL
A885 LD A,H
A886 POP HL
A887 POP DE
A888 RET
A889: SAVE_REGS_FRAME / RESTORE_REGS_FRAME -- paired full CPU-context save and restore.
save_regs_frame pushes main+alternate register sets, IX, IY (EX AF,AF'/EXX to reach the shadow bank), then reads a 16-bit value out of the saved frame ($SP+$17) into HL before returning.
restore_regs_frame ($A8A5) pops them all back.
i8080: no shadow regs/IX/IY on 8080 -- a port saves only the real register set drop the EXX/EX AF,AF' halves.
save_regs_frame A889 DEFB $E3,$D5,$C5,$F5,$08,$D9,$E5,$D5
A891 DEFB $C5,$F5,$08,$D9,$DD,$E5,$FD,$E5
A899 DEFB $E5,$F5,$21,$17,$00,$39,$7E
A8A0 DEFM "+ng"
A8A3: Data block at A8A3
A8A3 DEFB $F1,$C9,$E1,$FD,$E1,$DD,$E1,$08
A8AB DEFB $D9,$F1,$C1,$D1,$E1,$08,$D9,$F1
A8B3 DEFB $C1,$D1,$E3,$C9,$00,$05,$00,$06
A8BB DEFB $00,$07,$00,$08,$00,$09,$00,$10
A8C3 DEFB $00,$11,$00,$12
str_player1 A8C7 DEFM "PLAYER 1"
A8CF: Unused
A8CF DEFS $01
str_player2 A8D0 DEFM "PLAYER 2"
A8D8: Data block at A8D8
A8D8 DEFB $00,$08,$04,$02,$00,$0C,$06,$02
A8E0 DEFB $00,$08,$04,$08,$07,$10,$06,$08
A8E8 DEFB $07,$20,$00
A8EB: SETUP_ATTRS -- paint the static playfield attribute layout and the two PLAYER score labels.
Used by the routine at jp_save_regs.
Run once at start (via jp_setup_attrs).
Reads attr_rle (9 run-length [count,attr] pairs) and floods the attribute area $5800+ with attr|$40 (BRIGHT set on every cell).
Then prints the PLAYER 1 / PLAYER 2 strings and seeds two score/counter widgets via helper calls.
In: nothing.
Out: attribute area painted.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
setup_attrs A8EB LD A,$FF A = $FF
A8ED LD ($60C8),A store $FF to a flag at $60C8
A8F0 LD HL,$A8D9 HL -> attr_rle (run-length attribute table)
A8F3 LD DE,$5800 DE -> $5800 = attribute area base
A8F6 LD C,$09 C = 9 outer groups
setup_attrs_0 A8F8 LD B,(HL) B = run length for this group
A8F9 INC HL
A8FA LD A,(HL) A = attribute byte for this group
A8FB OR $40 force BRIGHT bit ($40) on
A8FD INC HL
setup_attrs_1 A8FE LD (DE),A write attr to cell
A8FF INC DE
A900 DJNZ setup_attrs_1
A902 DEC C
A903 JR NZ,setup_attrs_0 next group until C groups done
A905 LD HL,$A8C7 HL -> 'PLAYER 1' string
A908 LD BC,$0000 BC = 0x0000 = attr/screen position arg
A90B CALL print_string print string at position (helper $AB53)
A90E LD BC,$1800 BC = 0x1800 = position for 'PLAYER 2'
A911 CALL print_string
A914 LD BC,$0900
A917 LD A,$A8
A919 CALL $AAC2
A91C LD BC,$1600
A91F LD A,$A7
A921 CALL $AAC2
A924 LD BC,$0002
A927 CALL draw_hud_bar
A92A LD BC,$1802
A92D CALL draw_hud_bar
A930 CALL reset_score_state draw remaining score widgets
A933 JP draw_hud_counters tail-jump into the next setup step
A936: DRAW_HUD_BAR -- at the print position in BC, print 8 copies of glyph $81 (a solid block) -- the score-panel divider bar.
Used by the routine at setup_attrs.
In: BC=print pos.
Clobbers: A,B.
draw_hud_bar A936 CALL set_print_pos
A939 LD A,$81
A93B LD B,$08
draw_hud_bar_0 A93D CALL print_char
A940 DJNZ draw_hud_bar_0
A942 RET
A943: RESET_SCORE_STATE -- init the score/lives HUD state for a new game: lives_p2 ($6041)=3, $6042=3, $60C2=0, and zero the 8-byte block $6043..$604A (the two 4-byte BCD score buffers).
Used by the routines at jp_reset_score and setup_attrs.
Reached from proc_table slot $A721 (JP) and from $A930 (CALL, then falls through to draw_hud_counters).
reset_score_state A943 LD A,$03
A945 LD ($6041),A
A948 LD ($6042),A
A94B XOR A
A94C LD ($60C2),A
A94F LD HL,$6043
A952 LD B,$08
reset_score_state_0 A954 LD (HL),A
A955 INC HL
A956 DJNZ reset_score_state_0
A958 RET
A959: DRAW_HUD_COUNTERS -- paint the score/lives panel.
Used by the routines at jp_draw_hud and setup_attrs.
Sets the '1UP'/'2UP' marker attribute cells (toggled by lives_p1), then prints lives_p2/$6042 as 2-digit decimals and the two BCD score buffers via set_print_pos + print_bcd_byte.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
draw_hud_counters A959 LD A,$FF
A95B LD ($60C8),A
A95E LD HL,$0046
A961 LD A,($6040)
A964 OR A
A965 JR Z,draw_hud_counters_0
A967 LD HL,$4600
draw_hud_counters_0 A96A LD A,L
A96B LD ($5809),A
A96E LD A,H
A96F LD ($5816),A
A972 LD HL,$5840
A975 LD A,($6041)
A978 CALL draw_gauge8
A97B LD HL,$5858
A97E LD A,($6042)
A981 CALL draw_gauge8
A984 LD BC,$0001
A987 LD HL,$6046
A98A CALL print_score_field
A98D LD BC,$1801
A990 LD HL,$604A
A993: PRINT_SCORE_FIELD -- position the cursor (BC) then print a 2-byte (4-digit) BCD value at HL via print_bcd_word.
Used by the routine at draw_hud_counters.
In: BC=pos, HL=MSB.
print_score_field A993 CALL set_print_pos
A996 CALL print_bcd_word
print_bcd_word A999 CALL print_bcd_byte
print_bcd_byte A99C LD A,(HL)
A99D RRA
A99E RRA
A99F RRA
A9A0 RRA
A9A1 CALL print_nibble
A9A4 LD A,(HL)
A9A5 DEC HL
print_nibble A9A6 AND $0F
A9A8 ADD A,$30
A9AA JP print_char
A9AD: Routine at A9AD
Used by the routine at draw_hud_counters.
draw_gauge8 A9AD LD B,$08
A9AF LD C,A
draw_gauge8_0 A9B0 LD A,$00
A9B2 DEC C
A9B3 JP M,draw_gauge8_1
A9B6 LD A,$42
draw_gauge8_1 A9B8 LD (HL),A
A9B9 INC HL
A9BA DJNZ draw_gauge8_0
A9BC RET
A9BD: ADD_BCD_SCORE -- add HL (16-bit BCD delta) to the active player's 4-byte BCD score (lives_p1 selects $6043 or $6047), with DAA carry propagation across all 4 bytes.
Used by the routine at jp_add_score.
In: HL=BCD delta, lives_p1=index.
Clobbers: A,B,C,DE.
i8080: DAA exists but only valid after ADD/ADC (as used here).
add_bcd_score A9BD LD DE,$6043
A9C0 LD A,($6040)
A9C3 OR A
A9C4 JR Z,add_bcd_score_0
A9C6 LD DE,$6047
add_bcd_score_0 A9C9 LD A,(DE)
A9CA ADD A,L
A9CB DAA
A9CC LD (DE),A
A9CD INC DE
A9CE LD A,(DE)
A9CF LD B,A
A9D0 ADC A,H
A9D1 DAA
A9D2 LD (DE),A
A9D3 LD C,A
A9D4 INC DE
A9D5 LD A,(DE)
A9D6 ADC A,$00
A9D8 LD (DE),A
A9D9 INC DE
A9DA LD A,(DE)
A9DB ADC A,$00
A9DD LD (DE),A
A9DE LD A,B
A9DF AND $F0
A9E1 CP $40
A9E3 JR NZ,add_bcd_score_1
A9E5 LD A,C
A9E6 AND $F0
A9E8 CP $50
A9EA JR Z,add_bcd_score_2
add_bcd_score_1 A9EC LD A,B
A9ED AND $F0
A9EF CP $90
A9F1 RET NZ
A9F2 LD A,C
A9F3 AND $F0
A9F5 RET NZ
add_bcd_score_2 A9F6 LD HL,$6041
A9F9 LD A,($6040)
A9FC OR A
A9FD JR Z,add_bcd_score_3
A9FF INC HL
add_bcd_score_3 AA00 INC (HL)
AA01 LD A,(HL)
AA02 CP $08
AA04 RET C
AA05 LD (HL),$08
AA07 RET
AA08: INIT_BONUS -- seed the active player's (lives_p1 index) bonus/ accumulator block at $604B: bonus = BCD 0100, $604F-side cleared.
Used by the routine at jp_init_bonus.
Clobbers: A,HL.
init_bonus AA08 LD HL,$604B
AA0B LD A,($6040)
AA0E OR A
AA0F JR Z,init_bonus_0
AA11 INC HL
AA12 INC HL
init_bonus_0 AA13 LD (HL),$00
AA15 INC HL
AA16 LD (HL),$01
AA18 INC HL
AA19 INC HL
AA1A INC HL
AA1B LD (HL),$00
AA1D INC HL
AA1E LD (HL),$00
AA20 RET
AA21: BONUS_SUB -- BCD-subtract B from the active player's bonus value ($604B, 2 bytes), clamping at 0 on underflow (RET NC else zero it).
Used by the routine at jp_bonus_sub.
In: A/B=BCD amount.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
i8080: DAA after SUB/SBC.
bonus_sub AA21 LD B,A
AA22 LD HL,$604B
AA25 LD A,($6040)
AA28 OR A
AA29 JR Z,bonus_sub_0
AA2B INC HL
AA2C INC HL
bonus_sub_0 AA2D LD A,(HL)
AA2E SUB B
AA2F DAA
AA30 LD (HL),A
AA31 INC HL
AA32 LD A,(HL)
AA33 SBC A,$00
AA35 DAA
AA36 LD (HL),A
AA37 RET NC
AA38 XOR A
AA39 LD (HL),A
AA3A DEC HL
AA3B LD (HL),A
AA3C RET
AA3D: FIELD_604F_ADD -- BCD-add B to the active player's 2-byte field at $604F, saturating: high byte hitting 6 resets to 05/low=00.
Used by the routine at jp_field_604f_add.
In: A/B=BCD amount.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
field_604f_add AA3D LD B,A
AA3E LD HL,$604F
AA41 LD A,($6040)
AA44 OR A
AA45 JR Z,field_604f_add_0
AA47 INC HL
AA48 INC HL
field_604f_add_0 AA49 LD A,(HL)
AA4A ADD A,B
AA4B DAA
AA4C LD (HL),A
AA4D INC HL
AA4E LD A,(HL)
AA4F ADC A,$00
AA51 LD (HL),A
AA52 CP $06
AA54 RET C
AA55 LD (HL),$05
AA57 DEC HL
AA58 LD (HL),$00
AA5A RET
AA5B: SET_LEVEL_TIMER -- load play_timer from the word table at $A8B7 indexed by $6039*2 (per-level start time), then fall into draw_timer to show it.
Used by the routine at jp_set_level_timer.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
set_level_timer AA5B LD A,($6039)
AA5E ADD A,A
AA5F LD E,A
AA60 LD D,$00
AA62 LD HL,$A8B7
AA65 ADD HL,DE
AA66 LD E,(HL)
AA67 INC HL
AA68 LD D,(HL)
AA69 LD ($6053),DE
AA6D JP draw_timer
AA70: TICK_TIMER -- prescaled level-timer countdown.
Used by the routine at jp_tick_timer.
$6055 counts 0..4 only every 5th call does it BCD-decrement play_timer (2 bytes, clamped at 0) and redraw via draw_timer.
RET M while prescaler is negative (timer disabled).
Clobbers: A,HL.
tick_timer AA70 LD HL,$6055
AA73 LD A,(HL)
AA74 OR A
AA75 RET M
AA76 INC A
AA77 LD (HL),A
AA78 CP $05
AA7A RET C
AA7B LD (HL),$00
AA7D LD HL,$6053
AA80 LD A,(HL)
AA81 SUB $01
AA83 DAA
AA84 LD (HL),A
AA85 INC HL
AA86 LD A,(HL)
AA87 SBC A,$00
AA89 DAA
AA8A LD (HL),A
AA8B JP P,draw_timer
AA8E XOR A
AA8F LD (HL),A
AA90 DEC HL
AA91 LD (HL),A
AA92 DEC A
AA93 LD ($6055),A
AA96: DRAW_TIMER -- print the 2-byte BCD play_timer ($6054) as decimal digits at print row $0E (white).
Used by the routines at jp_draw_timer, set_level_timer and tick_timer.
Uses print_2digits/print_digit.
Clobbers: A,BC,HL.
draw_timer AA96 LD A,$FF
AA98 LD ($60C8),A
AA9B LD BC,$0E00
AA9E CALL set_print_pos
AAA1 LD HL,$6054
AAA4 CALL print_2digits
AAA7 DEC HL
print_2digits AAA8 LD A,(HL)
AAA9 RRA
AAAA RRA
AAAB RRA
AAAC RRA
AAAD CALL print_digit
AAB0 LD A,(HL)
print_digit AAB1 AND $0F
AAB3 ADD A,$30
AAB5 JP print_char
AAB8: Data block at AAB8
punct_map AAB8 DEFB $20,$00
AABA DEFM "<(>'"
AABE: Data block at AABE
AABE DEFB $40,$26,$2A,$19,$CD,$7A,$AB
AAC5: PRINT_CHAR -- render one character at the current text cursor.
Used by the routines at jp_print_char, draw_hud_bar, print_score_field, draw_timer and print_string_loop.
In: A = character code.
>=$80 : direct glyph index (graphics char), A&$7F.
'A'..'Z' : glyph = (A-'A')+2.
'0'..'9' : glyph = (A-'0')+$1C.
<$10 : colour/attribute control code -> cur_color.
else : looked up in punct_map.
cursor in print_cursor ($60C6 lo=col) cur_color ($60C8).
Out: glyph blitted to screen column cursor ($60C7) advanced.
print_char AAC5 PUSH AF save all regs (caller-transparent)
AAC6 PUSH BC
AAC7 PUSH DE
AAC8 PUSH HL
AAC9 BIT 7,A bit7 set? -> direct graphics glyph index
AACB JR Z,print_char_0
AACD AND $7F strip bit7, use as glyph index
AACF JR glyph_addr
print_char_0 AAD1 CP $41 'A'($41)..'Z'($5A)?
AAD3 JR C,print_char_1
AAD5 CP $5B
AAD7 JR NC,print_char_1
AAD9 SUB $41 glyph = (A-'A') + 2 (letters start at glyph 2)
AADB ADD A,$02
AADD JR glyph_addr
print_char_1 AADF CP $30 '0'($30)..'9'($39)?
AAE1 JR C,print_char_2
AAE3 CP $3A
AAE5 JR NC,print_char_2
AAE7 SUB $30 glyph = (A-'0') + $1C (digits start at glyph $1C)
AAE9 ADD A,$1C
AAEB JR glyph_addr
print_char_2 AAED CP $10 code <$10? -> colour control code
AAEF JR NC,print_char_4
AAF1 LD B,A
AAF2 AND $07 ink = A & 7
AAF4 BIT 3,B bit3 of code = BRIGHT -> OR $40
AAF6 JR Z,print_char_3
AAF8 OR $40
print_char_3 AAFA LD ($60C8),A store into cur_color ($60C8)
AAFD JR print_char_9
print_char_4 AAFF LD HL,$AAB8 HL -> punct_map (5 entries)
AB02 LD B,$05
print_char_5 AB04 CP (HL) compare code against each map char
AB05 JR Z,print_char_6
AB07 INC HL
AB08 INC HL
AB09 DJNZ print_char_5
AB0B JR print_char_9
print_char_6 AB0D INC HL
AB0E LD A,(HL) matched: A = glyph index from map
glyph_addr AB0F LD L,A L = glyph index
AB10 ADD A,A A = index*6 via index + index*2, then *2 (6 bytes/glyph)
AB11 ADD A,L
AB12 ADD A,A
AB13 LD L,A HL = index*6
AB14 LD H,$00
AB16 LD DE,$C000 DE = $C000 = font base
AB19 ADD HL,DE HL -> glyph bitmap (6 bytes)
AB1A PUSH HL
AB1B LD BC,($60C6) BC = print_cursor (C=column 0..31)
glyph_blit AB1F LD A,C A = column
AB20 AND $18 H = (col & $18) | $40 : screen-addr high byte (third+page)
AB22 LD H,A
AB23 SET 6,H
AB25 RRCA
AB26 RRCA
AB27 RRCA
AB28 OR $58 OR $58 -> D = matching attribute-area high byte ($58xx)
AB2A LD D,A
AB2B LD A,C
AB2C AND $07
AB2E RRCA
AB2F RRCA
AB30 RRCA
AB31 ADD A,B
AB32 LD L,A
AB33 LD E,A
AB34 LD A,($60C8) A = cur_color
AB37 CP $FF $FF = 'transparent' -> skip attribute write
AB39 JR Z,print_char_7
AB3B LD (DE),A write colour to attribute cell
print_char_7 AB3C POP DE
AB3D LD (HL),$00 blank top scanline of the cell
AB3F INC H INC H = step down one pixel row (Z80 ZX adjacency)
AB40 LD B,$06 B = 6 font rows
print_char_8 AB42 LD A,(DE) copy one font row to screen
AB43 LD (HL),A
AB44 INC DE
AB45 INC H
AB46 DJNZ print_char_8
AB48 LD (HL),$00 blank bottom scanline
AB4A LD HL,$60C7
AB4D INC (HL) advance column cursor ($60C7)
print_char_9 AB4E POP HL
AB4F POP DE
AB50 POP BC
AB51 POP AF
AB52 RET
AB53: PRINT_STRING -- print an ASCIIZ string at position BC.
Used by the routine at setup_attrs.
In: HL=string (00-terminated), BC=cursor pos.
Out: HL past terminator.
Uses print_char per byte.
print_string AB53 CALL set_print_pos set cursor = BC
AB56: Routine at AB56
Used by the routine at print_centered.
print_string_loop AB56 LD A,(HL) A = next char
AB57 INC HL
AB58 OR A 00 terminator?
AB59 RET Z done (ASCIIZ) -- RET on Z
AB5A CALL print_char print it
AB5D JR print_string_loop next char
AB5F: Routine at AB5F
Used by the routine at jp_print_centered.
print_centered AB5F PUSH HL
AB60 LD C,$00 C = visible-char count
print_centered_0 AB62 LD A,(HL)
AB63 OR A
AB64 JR Z,print_centered_2
AB66 CP $10 ignore codes <$10 (colour controls) in the count
AB68 JR C,print_centered_1
AB6A INC C
print_centered_1 AB6B INC HL
AB6C JR print_centered_0
print_centered_2 AB6E LD A,$20 A = 32 - count
AB70 SUB C
AB71 RRA RRA = /2 = left margin
AB72 LD ($60C7),A store as start column ($60C7)
AB75 POP HL
AB76 CALL print_string_loop
AB79 RET
AB7A: Routine at AB7A
Used by the routines at draw_hud_bar, print_score_field, draw_timer and print_string.
set_print_pos AB7A LD ($60C6),BC
AB7E RET
AB7F: Routine at AB7F
Used by the routine at setup_tower_scroll.
clear_obj_table AB7F LD HL,$6460
AB82 LD DE,$000D
AB85 LD B,$14
AB87 XOR A
clear_obj_table_0 AB88 LD (HL),A
AB89 INC HL
AB8A LD (HL),A
AB8B INC HL
AB8C LD (HL),A
AB8D ADD HL,DE
AB8E LD (HL),A
AB8F INC HL
AB90 LD (HL),A
AB91 INC HL
AB92 LD (HL),A
AB93 INC HL
AB94 DJNZ clear_obj_table_0
AB96 RET

calc_tower_col_addr AB97 LD A,($6032)

AB9D ADD A,A

AB9F ADD A,A
ABA0 ADD A,A

ABAB CP $60

ABAF SUB $60
calc_tower_col_addr_0 ABB1 LD C,A

ABB5 ADD HL,BC
ABB6 LD A,($6030)
ABB9 AND $04

ABBD EX DE,HL
calc_tower_col_addr_1 ABBE PUSH DE
ABBF LD DE,$6463
ABC2 LDI
ABC4 LDI

ABCC LDI

calc_tower_col_addr_2 ABFE LDI
AC00 LDI
AC02 LDI
AC04 LDI

AC08 LDI
AC0A LDI
AC0C LDI

Used by the routine at jp_col_visible.
Returns A=offset (RET Z if off-screen).

col_visible_offset AC22 LD HL,($60BA)

AC26 SUB $28
AC28 LD L,A
AC29 LD A,H
AC2A SBC A,$00

AC2F LD B,$00

AC36 SUB E

AC38 LD A,H

col_visible_offset_1 AC52 LD HL,$6030

AC56 LD (HL),A

AC58 INC HL
AC59 ADC A,(HL)
AC5A AND $03

AC5D RET
AC5E: BLIT_SCROLL_WRAP -- when scroll_pos has wrapped past the top (H==$03 && L>=$D8), fast-fill the newly exposed strip using the LD SP,screen / stack-write trick (saves real SP to $6000, DI, reads mask_acb0).
Used by the routine at jp_blit_scroll_wrap.
RET early when no wrap is pending.
i8080: 8080 has no shadow regs and SP push-blit is far slower a port should draw this strip with ordinary stores.
blit_scroll_wrap AC5E LD HL,($6030)
AC61 LD A,H
AC62 CP $03
AC64 RET NZ
AC65 LD A,L
AC66 CP $D8
AC68 RET C
AC69 XOR A
AC6A SUB L
AC6B SUB L
AC6C PUSH AF
AC6D LD B,A
AC6E LD A,$B8
AC70 SUB B
AC71 LD H,A
AC72 LD L,$1E
AC74 CALL get_screen_addr
AC77 POP AF
AC78 LD ($6000),SP
AC7C DI
AC7D LD DE,$ACB0
blit_scroll_wrap_0 AC80 EX AF,AF'
AC81 LD SP,HL
AC82 EX DE,HL
AC83 LD C,(HL)
AC84 INC HL
AC85 LD B,C
AC86 PUSH BC
AC87 PUSH BC
AC88 PUSH BC
AC89 PUSH BC
AC8A PUSH BC
AC8B PUSH BC
AC8C PUSH BC
AC8D PUSH BC
AC8E PUSH BC
AC8F PUSH BC
AC90 PUSH BC
AC91 PUSH BC
AC92 PUSH BC
AC93 PUSH BC

AC95 INC H
AC96 LD A,H
AC97 AND $07
AC99 JR NZ,blit_scroll_wrap_1
AC9B LD A,L
AC9C ADD A,$20
AC9E LD L,A
AC9F JR C,blit_scroll_wrap_1
ACA1 LD A,H
ACA2 SUB $08
ACA4 LD H,A
blit_scroll_wrap_1 ACA5 EX AF,AF'
ACA6 DEC A
ACA7 JP NZ,blit_scroll_wrap_0
ACAA LD SP,($6000)
ACAE EI
ACAF RET

AD82: SETUP_TOWER_SCROLL -- per-frame scroll/render setup.
Used by the routine at jp_setup_scroll.
Clears the object table, derives the coarse column ($ADC6) and sub-column fraction ($6021) from scroll_pos, and the row base ($AD61) from disp_height.

B346: LOAD_LEVEL_COL -- fetch column descriptor #$6039 from the column-major table at $7A00 (stride 8): low byte (+0) and high byte (+8) form a pointer (offset by $6A00 into level data), and the property byte (+16) is stashed in $603A.
Used by the routine at jp_load_level_col.
Returns IX=pointer.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL,IX.
load_level_col B346 LD A,($6039)
B349 LD C,A
B34A ADD A,A
B34B ADD A,$6A
B34D LD D,A
B34E LD E,$00
B350 LD B,$00
B352 LD IX,$7A00
B356 ADD IX,BC
B358 LD L,(IX+$00)
B35B LD H,(IX+$08)
B35E LD BC,$6A00
B361 ADD HL,BC
B362 LD A,(IX+$10)
B365 LD ($603A),A
B368 PUSH HL
B369 POP IX
B36B LD C,A
B36C LD HL,$F000

B377 INC L
B378 DEC C
B379 JR NZ,load_level_col_0
B37B LD HL,$F000
B37E LD ($6015),HL
B381 RET
B382: BLIT_MASKED_COLUMN -- draw an 8-pixel-tall column at HL: for each set bit of the mask byte at (DE), store the next source byte (IX++) and step down one pixel row (INC H) skip rows whose mask bit is 0.
Used by the routine at load_level_col.
In: DE=mask ptr, IX=pixels, HL=screen.
Clobbers: A,B,HL,IX.
blit_masked_column B382 LD A,(DE)
B383 INC DE
B384 LD B,$08
blit_masked_column_0 B386 RRA
B387 JR NC,blit_masked_column_1
B389 PUSH AF
B38A LD A,(IX+$00)
B38D INC IX
B38F LD (HL),A
B390 POP AF
blit_masked_column_1 B391 INC H
B392 DJNZ blit_masked_column_0
B394 RET
B395: ALLOC_OBJECT_SLOT -- find a free entry (marker $FF at +0) in the 4-slot active-object table at $60A9.
Used by the routine at jp_alloc_slot.
On success store its type ($7D), source pointer (HL) and a copied data byte, set CY, RET.
In: HL=source.
Out: CY=allocated.
Clobbers: A,B,IX.
alloc_object_slot B395 LD IX,$60A9
B399 LD B,$04
alloc_object_slot_0 B39B LD A,(IX+$00)
B39E CP $FF
B3A0 JR NZ,alloc_object_slot_1
B3A2 LD A,(HL)
B3A3 AND $0C
B3A5 CP $04
B3A7 JR Z,alloc_object_slot_2
B3A9 LD A,(HL)
B3AA LD (IX+$0C),A
B3AD LD (IX+$04),L
B3B0 LD (IX+$08),H
B3B3 LD (IX+$00),$7D
B3B7 SCF
B3B8 RET
alloc_object_slot_1 B3B9 INC IX
B3BB DJNZ alloc_object_slot_0
alloc_object_slot_2 B3BD OR A
B3BE RET
B3BF: UPDATE_OBJECT_SLOTS -- tick the 4-entry active-object table at $60A9 (counterpart to alloc_object_slot).
Used by the routine at jp_update_objects.
For each slot: $FF=free (skip), >1 = decrement its countdown, and on expiry write the stored byte back through its screen pointer (erase the object).
Clobbers: A,B,HL,IX.
update_object_slots B3BF LD IX,$60A9
B3C3 LD B,$04
update_object_slots_0 B3C5 LD A,(IX+$00)
B3C8 OR A
B3C9 JR Z,update_object_slots_1

B3CF DEC (IX+$00)
B3D2 JR update_object_slots_3
