B3D7 LD H,(IX+$08)
B3DA LD A,(IX+$0C)
B3DD AND $0C
B3DF LD (HL),A
B3E0 DEC L
B3E1 DEC (IX+$04)
B3E4 LD A,(HL)
B3E5 LD (IX+$0C),A
B3E8 AND $0C
B3EA CP $04
B3EC JR Z,update_object_slots_2
B3EE LD (HL),$8D
B3F0 JP update_object_slots_3
update_object_slots_2 B3F3 LD (HL),$85
B3F5 LD (IX+$00),$FF
update_object_slots_3 B3F9 INC IX
B3FB DJNZ update_object_slots_0
B3FD RET
B3FE: FREE_OBJECT_SLOT -- mark the $60A9 slot whose stored pointer equals HL as free (set +0 = $FF).
Used by the routine at jp_free_object.
In: HL=pointer.
Clobbers: A,B,IX.
free_object_slot B3FE LD IX,$60A9
B402 LD B,$04
free_object_slot_0 B404 LD A,(IX+$00)
B407 CP $FF
B409 JR Z,free_object_slot_1
B40B LD A,L
B40C CP (IX+$04)
B40F JR NZ,free_object_slot_1
B411 LD A,H
B412 CP (IX+$08)
B415 JR NZ,free_object_slot_1
B417 LD (IX+$00),$FF
B41B RET
free_object_slot_1 B41C INC IX
B41E DJNZ free_object_slot_0
B420 RET
B421: UPDATE_ACTORS -- per-frame actor pass (proc_table slot A76F).
Used by the routine at jp_update_actors.
Skips when player_state is 8/9 (level-end).
Derives the camera row $6036 from $6059, then walks the 4 object_table actors, acting on each live one (state field IX+48 != 0).
Clobbers: A,B,HL,IX.
update_actors B421 LD A,($60C0)
B424 CP $08
B426 RET Z
B427 CP $09
B429 RET Z
B42A LD HL,($6059)
B42D LD A,L
B42E RR H
B430 RRA
B431 RR H
B433 RRA
B434 LD ($6036),A
B437 LD IX,$605B
B43B LD B,$04
update_actors_0 B43D LD A,(IX+$30)
B440 CP $00
B442 JR Z,update_actors_1
B444 INC IX
B446 DJNZ update_actors_0
B448 RET
update_actors_1 B449 LD HL,($6015)
B44C LD A,($603A)
B44F CP L
B450 JP Z,update_actors_14
B453 LD A,($6036)
B456 CP L
B457 JP Z,update_actors_14
B45A JP C,update_actors_14
update_actors_2 B45D LD A,(HL)
B45E OR A
B45F JP M,update_actors_3
B462 AND $70
B464 JR NZ,update_actors_4
update_actors_3 B466 LD A,H
B467 SUB $F0
B469 INC A
B46A AND $0F
B46C ADD A,$F0
B46E LD H,A
B46F CP $F0
B471 JR NZ,update_actors_2
B473 INC L
B474 LD ($6015),HL
B477 RET
update_actors_4 B478 LD ($6015),HL
B47B LD A,(HL)
B47C PUSH AF
B47D PUSH HL
B47E LD A,H
B47F SUB $F0
B481 INC A
B482 AND $0F
B484 ADD A,$F0
B486 LD H,A
B487 CP $F0
B489 JR NZ,update_actors_5
B48B INC L
update_actors_5 B48C LD ($6015),HL
B48F POP HL
B490 POP AF
B491 AND $70
B493 CP $10
B495 JR Z,update_actors_12
B497 CP $60
B499 JR NC,update_actors_10
B49B CP $40
B49D JR NC,update_actors_8
B49F CP $20
B4A1 JR Z,update_actors_6
B4A3 XOR A
B4A4 JR update_actors_7
update_actors_6 B4A6 LD A,$01
update_actors_7 B4A8 LD (IX+$36),A
B4AB LD (IX+$42),$01
B4AF LD (IX+$3C),$00
B4B3 JP update_actors_13
update_actors_8 B4B6 AND $10
B4B8 JR Z,update_actors_9
B4BA LD A,$01
update_actors_9 B4BC INC A
B4BD LD (IX+$36),A
B4C0 LD (IX+$42),$08
B4C4 LD (IX+$3C),$00
B4C8 JP update_actors_13
update_actors_10 B4CB AND $10
B4CD JR Z,update_actors_11
B4CF LD A,$01
update_actors_11 B4D1 INC A
B4D2 LD (IX+$36),A
B4D5 LD (IX+$42),$09
B4D9 LD (IX+$3C),$00
B4DD JP update_actors_13
update_actors_12 B4E0 LD A,$01
B4E2 LD (IX+$36),A
B4E5 LD (IX+$42),$02
B4E9 LD (IX+$3C),$00
update_actors_13 B4ED LD A,H
B4EE SUB $F0
B4F0 ADD A,A
B4F1 ADD A,A
B4F2 ADD A,A
B4F3 ADD A,$04
B4F5 AND $7F
B4F7 LD (IX+$00),A
B4FA XOR A
B4FB RL L
B4FD RL A
B4FF RL L
B501 RL A
B503 LD (IX+$06),L
B506 LD (IX+$0C),A
B509 LD (IX+$30),$06
B50D LD (IX+$2A),$00
B511 RET
update_actors_14 B512 LD A,($60A3)
B515 CP $FF
B517 RET Z
B518 DEC A
B519 LD ($60A3),A
B51C CP $FF
B51E RET NZ
B51F LD (IX+$30),$07
B523 LD (IX+$2A),$00
B527 LD A,($60A4)
B52A NEG
B52C LD ($60A4),A
B52F LD (IX+$36),A
B532 ADD A,A
B533 LD A,$00
B535 JR NC,update_actors_15
B537 LD A,$78
update_actors_15 B539 LD (IX+$00),A
B53C LD HL,($60BA)
B53F LD (IX+$06),L
B542 LD (IX+$0C),H
B545 RET
B546: CLEAR_SCREEN -- cold wipe before the title/play screen draws.
Used by the routine at jp_clear_screen.
1) zero game RAM $6000..$61FF (512 bytes, 2 pages).
2) zero the entire display $4000..$5AFF (pixels+attrs) via LDIR.
3) CALL $B567 (build a lookup table in the reclaimed printer buffer $5B00) and $B5EA.
In: nothing.
Out: screen+vars cleared.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
i8080 PORT: LDIR is a Z80 block fill -- expand to an explicit DCR/JNZ store loop.
The $6000 clear uses INC L / INC H pagi
clear_screen B546 LD HL,$6000 HL -> game RAM base $6000
B549 LD B,$02 B = 2 pages to clear
clear_screen_0 B54B LD (HL),$00 store 0
B54D INC L
B54E JR NZ,clear_screen_0 loop 256 bytes of this page
B550 INC H next page
B551 DJNZ clear_screen_0
B553 LD HL,$4000 HL -> screen base $4000
B556 LD DE,$4001 DE -> $4001 (LDIR src=HL, dst=DE, overlap fill)
B559 LD BC,$1AFF BC = $1AFF = 6911 bytes = whole display minus 1
B55C LD (HL),$00 seed first byte = 0
B55E LDIR LDIR: propagate 0 across $4000..$5AFF (Z80 block op)
B560 CALL build_screen_hi_lut build pixel/attr lookup table in $5B00
B563 CALL build_row_addr_table
B566 RET
B567: BUILD_SCREEN_HI_LUT -- fill a 256-byte table at $5B00 mapping a byte value to its bit-reordered form used in ZX screen-address math.
Used by the routines at jp_clear_screen and clear_screen.
Processes the index in 2-bit groups groups 00 and 11 pass through, 01/10 get +$40 -- the Y2<->Y0 swap that makes the ZX pixel rows non-linear.
Result accumulates in H.
i8080 PORT: the Specialist framebuffer is LINEAR -- this whole table collapses to identity.
The porter can DELETE this and the $5B00 lookups, replac
build_screen_hi_lut B567 LD DE,$5B00 DE -> $5B00 LUT base (reclaimed printer buffer)
build_screen_hi_lut_0 B56A LD C,E C = current index (low byte of DE)
B56B LD B,$04 B = 4 groups of 2 bits
build_screen_hi_lut_1 B56D LD A,C
B56E AND $C0 isolate the top 2-bit group
B570 JR Z,build_screen_hi_lut_2 00 -> pass through
B572 CP $C0
B574 JR Z,build_screen_hi_lut_2 11 -> pass through
B576 ADD A,$40 01/10 -> +$40 (the scan-order bit swap)
build_screen_hi_lut_2 B578 LD L,A
B579 ADD HL,HL HL <<= 2 (accumulate this group)
B57A ADD HL,HL
B57B SLA C shift C left 2 to expose next group (Z80 SLA)
B57D SLA C
B57F DJNZ build_screen_hi_lut_1
B581 LD A,H
B582 LD (DE),A store H as LUT[index]
B583 INC E
B584 JR NZ,build_screen_hi_lut_0 loop all 256 entries (E wraps to 0)
B586 RET
B587: Routine at B587
Used by the routine at jp_clear_playfield.
clear_playfield B587 LD C,$20
clear_playfield_0 B589 LD A,C compute screen-addr high byte from row index
B58A RRA
B58B RRA
B58C RRA
B58D AND $18
B58F OR $40
B591 LD H,A
B592 LD A,C
B593 AND $07
B595 ADD A,H
B596 LD H,A
B597 LD A,C
B598 RLA
B599 RLA
B59A AND $E0
B59C LD L,A
B59D LD B,$20
clear_playfield_1 B59F LD (HL),$00 zero 32 pixel bytes of this row
B5A1 INC HL
B5A2 DJNZ clear_playfield_1
B5A4 INC C
B5A5 LD A,C
B5A6 CP $B8 stop at row $B8
B5A8 JR NZ,clear_playfield_0
B5AA LD HL,$5860
B5AD LD DE,$5861
B5B0 LD BC,$0020
B5B3 LD (HL),$00
B5B5 LDIR LDIR: clear an attribute strip (Z80 block op)
B5B7 LD BC,$025F
B5BA CALL lookup_col_attr
B5BD LD (HL),A
B5BE LDIR
B5C0 LD BC,$0020
B5C3 LD (HL),$00
B5C5 LDIR
B5C7 RET
B5C8: Data block at B5C8
hook_noop B5C8 DEFB $C9,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
B5CF: Routine at B5CF
Used by the routine at jp_init_b5cf.
init_b5cf B5CF LD A,$FF
B5D1 LD ($6038),A
B5D4 XOR A
B5D5 LD ($B5CB),A zero flag_b5cb at $B5CB (write into code padding)
B5D8 RET
B5D9: GET_SCREEN_ADDR -- pixel address for (row=H, col-in-row=L).
Used by the routines at jp_get_screen_addr, blit_scroll_wrap, select_player_sprite, draw_actor_sprite, draw_pending_sprite, setup_strip_draw and draw_tower_strip.
Indexes scr_row_addr_tbl ($5D00, 2 bytes/row) at 2*H to fetch the row base, then adds L to get the final byte address in HL.
In: H=row 0..191, L=byte offset within row.
Out: HL=screen addr.
Clobbers A preserves DE.
i8080 PORT: replace table lookup with row*32 + col on a linear framebuffer (no scan-order table needed).
get_screen_addr B5D9 PUSH DE
B5DA LD D,L
B5DB LD A,H
B5DC ADD A,A L = 2*H = table index
B5DD LD L,A
B5DE LD A,$5D H = $5D + carry -> HL -> scr_row_addr_tbl[H]
B5E0 ADC A,$00
B5E2 LD H,A
B5E3 LD A,D
B5E4 ADD A,(HL) A = row base low + col offset
B5E5 INC L
B5E6 LD H,(HL) H = row base high
B5E7 LD L,A
B5E8 POP DE
B5E9 RET
B5EA: BUILD_ROW_ADDR_TABLE -- precompute 192 screen-row addresses into scr_row_addr_tbl ($5D00, little-endian words).
Used by the routine at clear_screen.
For each row y in 0..191 it builds the ZX pixel address:
high = $40 | (y&7) | ((y>>3)&$18)
low = (y&$38)<<2 i.e.
the famous ZX scan-order layout (Y bits split across the address).
get_screen_addr then just indexes this table.
i8080 PORT: on a linear framebuffer this table is row*width -- trivial this builder can be dropped.
build_row_addr_table B5EA LD DE,$5D00 DE -> $5D00 table base
B5ED LD HL,$0000 HL: H = row counter y, builds address in HL
build_row_addr_table_0 B5F0 PUSH HL
B5F1 PUSH DE
B5F2 LD A,H (y>>3)&$18 -> D (the y bits 3..5 region)
B5F3 RRCA
B5F4 RRCA
B5F5 RRCA
B5F6 AND $18
B5F8 LD D,A
B5F9 LD A,H
B5FA AND $07
B5FC ADD A,D + (y&7) + $40 -> high byte
B5FD ADD A,$40
B5FF LD D,A
B600 LD A,H
B601 RLC A (y<<2)&$E0 -> low byte (E)
B603 RLC A
B605 AND $E0
B607 LD E,A
B608 LD H,$00
B60A ADD HL,DE HL = full row-0 screen address
B60B POP DE
B60C LD A,L
B60D LD (DE),A store LE word into table[y]
B60E INC DE
B60F LD A,H
B610 LD (DE),A
B611 INC DE
B612 POP HL
B613 INC H
B614 LD A,H
B615 CP $C0 stop after row $BF (192 rows)
B617 JP NZ,build_row_addr_table_0
B61A RET
B61B: GET_SPRITE_DEF -- look up sprite/graphic descriptor A in the gfx_c101 table (A*2): unpack the frame count (top 2 bits, 0->4) into C and the bitmap pointer (low 6 bits + $C1xx base) into HL.
Used by the routines at jp_get_sprite_def, select_player_sprite and draw_actor_sprite.
In: A=sprite id.
Out: HL=bitmap, C=frames.
Clobbers: A,HL.
get_sprite_def B61B ADD A,A
B61C LD L,A
B61D LD H,$00
B61F LD DE,$C101
B622 ADD HL,DE
B623 LD A,(HL)
B624 RLCA
B625 RLCA
B626 AND $03
B628 LD C,A
B629 JR NZ,get_sprite_def_0
B62B LD C,$04
get_sprite_def_0 B62D LD A,(HL)
B62E AND $3F
B630 ADD A,$C1
B632 DEC HL
B633 LD L,(HL)
B634 LD H,A
B635 RET
B636: SELECT_PLAYER_SPRITE -- pick the player's current sprite frame from $60C1 plus facing ($60BE bit7) and player_state, applying frame-base offsets ($1F walking-left / $44 walking-right).
Used by the routine at jp_select_sprite.
RET early if $60C2==0 (nothing to draw).
Clobbers: A,H,L.
select_player_sprite B636 LD A,($60C2)
B639 OR A
B63A RET Z
B63B LD A,($60C0)
B63E CP $08
B640 LD A,($60C1)
B643 JR NZ,select_player_sprite_1
B645 LD HL,$60BE
B648 BIT 7,(HL)
B64A JR Z,select_player_sprite_0
B64C ADD A,$1F
B64E JR select_player_sprite_3
select_player_sprite_0 B650 ADD A,$44
B652 JR select_player_sprite_3
select_player_sprite_1 B654 LD H,A
B655 AND $7F
B657 BIT 7,H
B659 JR NZ,select_player_sprite_2
B65B ADD A,$25
B65D JR select_player_sprite_3
select_player_sprite_2 B65F ADD A,$00
select_player_sprite_3 B661 CALL get_sprite_def
B664 PUSH HL
B665 PUSH BC
B666 LD A,($60BA)
B669 LD HL,$6030
B66C SUB (HL)
B66D ADD A,A
B66E LD C,A
B66F LD A,$A4
B671 SUB C
B672 LD H,A
B673 LD L,$0E
B675 LD ($B5C9),HL
B678 CALL get_screen_addr
B67B EX DE,HL
B67C POP BC
B67D POP HL
B67E LD ($6000),SP
B682 DI
B683 LD SP,HL
B684 EX DE,HL
B685 LD B,$0A
B687 BIT 2,C
B689 JP NZ,select_player_sprite_6
B68C INC L
select_player_sprite_4 B68D POP DE
B68E LD A,(HL)
B68F AND E
B690 OR D
B691 LD (HL),A
B692 INC L
B693 POP DE
B694 LD A,(HL)
B695 AND E
B696 OR D
B697 LD (HL),A
B698 DEC L
B699 INC H
B69A POP DE
B69B LD A,(HL)
B69C AND E
B69D OR D
B69E LD (HL),A
B69F INC L
B6A0 POP DE
B6A1 LD A,(HL)
B6A2 AND E
B6A3 OR D
B6A4 LD (HL),A
B6A5 DEC L
B6A6 INC H
B6A7 LD A,H
B6A8 AND $07
B6AA JP NZ,select_player_sprite_5
B6AD LD A,L
B6AE ADD A,$20
B6B0 LD L,A
B6B1 JR C,select_player_sprite_5
B6B3 LD A,H
B6B4 SUB $08
B6B6 LD H,A
select_player_sprite_5 B6B7 DJNZ select_player_sprite_4
B6B9 JP select_player_sprite_8
select_player_sprite_6 B6BC POP DE
B6BD LD C,L
B6BE LD A,(HL)
B6BF AND E
B6C0 LD (HL),A
B6C1 INC L
B6C2 LD A,(HL)
B6C3 AND D
B6C4 POP DE
B6C5 OR E
B6C6 LD (HL),A
B6C7 INC L
B6C8 LD A,(HL)
B6C9 AND D
B6CA POP DE
B6CB OR E
B6CC LD (HL),A
B6CD INC L
B6CE LD A,(HL)
B6CF AND D
B6D0 LD (HL),A
B6D1 LD L,C
B6D2 INC H
B6D3 POP DE
B6D4 LD A,(HL)
B6D5 AND E
B6D6 LD (HL),A
B6D7 INC L
B6D8 LD A,(HL)
B6D9 AND D
B6DA POP DE
B6DB OR E
B6DC LD (HL),A
B6DD INC L
B6DE LD A,(HL)
B6DF AND D
B6E0 POP DE
B6E1 OR E
B6E2 LD (HL),A
B6E3 INC L
B6E4 LD A,(HL)
B6E5 AND D
B6E6 LD (HL),A
B6E7 LD L,C
B6E8 INC H
B6E9 LD A,H
B6EA AND $07
B6EC JP NZ,select_player_sprite_7
B6EF LD A,L
B6F0 ADD A,$20
B6F2 LD L,A
B6F3 JR C,select_player_sprite_7
B6F5 LD A,H
B6F6 SUB $08
B6F8 LD H,A
select_player_sprite_7 B6F9 DJNZ select_player_sprite_6
select_player_sprite_8 B6FB LD SP,($6000)
B6FF EI
B700 LD A,($6038)
B703 CP $FF
B705 RET Z
B706 LD HL,($B5C9)
B709 LD A,H
B70A ADD A,$14
B70C LD H,A
B70D CALL get_screen_addr
B710 LD DE,$FC3F
B713 LD B,$00
B715 CALL plot_masked_row
B718 DEC B
B719 CALL plot_masked_row
B71C CALL plot_row_wrapped
B71F CALL plot_masked_row
B722 CALL plot_row_wrapped
B725 CALL plot_masked_row
B728 CALL plot_row_wrapped
B72B CALL plot_masked_row
B72E INC B
B72F: PLOT_ROW_WRAPPED -- advance the screen address HL down one pixel row (ZX scan-order arithmetic: char-cell wrap via +$20 / -$08) then fall into plot_masked_row.
Used by the routine at select_player_sprite.
Clobbers: A,HL (+ plot regs).
plot_row_wrapped B72F LD A,H
B730 AND $07
B732 JR NZ,plot_masked_row
B734 LD A,L
B735 ADD A,$20
B737 LD L,A
B738 JR C,plot_masked_row
B73A LD A,H
B73B SUB $08
B73D LD H,A
B73E: PLOT_MASKED_ROW -- plot a 3-cell sprite slice at HL: AND the left edge cell with mask D, write the two middle cells = B, AND the right edge with mask E restores L and bumps H.
Used by the routines at select_player_sprite and plot_row_wrapped.
In: HL,B,D,E.
Clobbers: A,C,HL.
plot_masked_row B73E LD C,L
B73F LD A,(HL)
B740 AND D
B741 LD (HL),A
B742 INC L
B743 LD (HL),B
B744 INC L
B745 LD (HL),B
B746 INC L
B747 LD A,(HL)
B748 AND E
B749 LD (HL),A
B74A LD L,C
B74B INC H
B74C RET
B74D: DRAW_ACTOR_SPRITE -- render one object_table actor (IX = entry).
Used by the routine at jp_draw_actor.
Skips if (IX+24)==$FF (inactive).
Computes screen-Y via actor_screen_y, special-cases state 7 (IX+48) to latch $60A6, bounds-checks Y to [$18,$B0), then draws.
Clobbers: A,HL,IX,...
draw_actor_sprite B74D LD A,(IX+$18)
B750 CP $FF
B752 RET Z
B753 CALL actor_screen_y
B756 LD A,(IX+$30)
B759 CP $07
B75B JR NZ,draw_actor_sprite_0
B75D LD A,(IX+$00)
B760 ADD A,A
B761 LD ($60A6),A
draw_actor_sprite_0 B764 LD A,($60A8)
B767 CP $18
B769 RET C
B76A CP $B0
B76C RET NC
B76D LD H,A
B76E LD A,($60A6)
B771 RRA
B772 RRA
B773 RRA
B774 AND $1F
B776 LD L,A
B777 LD A,(IX+$30)
B77A CP $07
B77C JR NZ,draw_actor_sprite_1
B77E LD ($B5CC),HL
B781 LD A,($60A6)
B784 SRL A
B786 AND $03
B788 LD ($B5CE),A
B78B LD A,$01
B78D LD ($B5CB),A
draw_actor_sprite_1 B790 CALL get_screen_addr
B793 PUSH HL
B794 LD A,(IX+$18)
B797 CP $7C
B799 JR C,draw_actor_sprite_3
B79B SUB $7C
B79D ADD A,A
B79E ADD A,A
B79F ADD A,A
B7A0 ADD A,A
B7A1 ADD A,A
B7A2 LD H,$00
B7A4 LD L,A
B7A5 ADD A,A
B7A6 ADD A,L
B7A7 LD L,A
B7A8 JR NC,draw_actor_sprite_2
B7AA INC H
draw_actor_sprite_2 B7AB LD DE,$BC80
B7AE ADD HL,DE
B7AF LD C,$03
B7B1 LD B,$08
B7B3 JR blit_sprite_fast_0
draw_actor_sprite_3 B7B5 LD C,A
B7B6 LD A,($60A6)
B7B9 SRL A
B7BB AND $03
B7BD ADD A,C
B7BE PUSH AF
B7BF CALL get_sprite_def
B7C2: BLIT_SPRITE_FAST -- fast sprite blit using the LD SP,screen / PUSH stack-write trick (saves the real SP to $6000, DI).
Height B is 8 or 4 (chosen by the $4E threshold) C selects a sub-variant.
i8080: the LD SP,screen / PUSH blit trick has no 8080 speed advantage and conflicts with interrupts -- port as normal stores.
blit_sprite_fast B7C2 POP AF
B7C3 LD B,$08
B7C5 CP $4E
B7C7 JR NC,blit_sprite_fast_0
B7C9 LD B,$04
This entry point is used by the routine at draw_actor_sprite.
blit_sprite_fast_0 B7CB POP DE
B7CC LD ($6000),SP
B7D0 DI
B7D1 LD SP,HL
B7D2 EX DE,HL
B7D3 DEC C
B7D4 JR Z,blit_sprite_fast_1
B7D6 DEC C
B7D7 JP Z,blit_sprite_fast_3
B7DA JP blit_sprite_fast_5
blit_sprite_fast_1 B7DD POP DE
B7DE LD A,(HL)
B7DF AND E
B7E0 OR D
B7E1 LD (HL),A
B7E2 INC H
B7E3 POP DE
B7E4 LD A,(HL)
B7E5 AND E
B7E6 OR D
B7E7 LD (HL),A
B7E8 INC H
B7E9 LD A,H
B7EA AND $07
B7EC JR NZ,blit_sprite_fast_2
B7EE LD A,L
B7EF ADD A,$20
B7F1 LD L,A
B7F2 JR C,blit_sprite_fast_2
B7F4 LD A,H
B7F5 SUB $08
B7F7 LD H,A
blit_sprite_fast_2 B7F8 DJNZ blit_sprite_fast_1
B7FA JP blit_sprite_fast_7
blit_sprite_fast_3 B7FD POP DE
B7FE LD A,(HL)
B7FF AND E

FC8A: TAPE_READ_BYTE -- read one byte from tape with the custom turbo edge-timing loop (ULA EAR bit via IN ($FE), border feedback via OUT ($FE)).
Used by the routine at tape_load_header.
Accumulates 8 bits CY=success, NC=load error/abort.
i8080: ZX-tape/ROM specific -- replaced wholesale by the Specialist's own program-load path not ported.

FC8B EX AF,AF'

FC8D DI

FD20: TAPE_GET_EDGE -- measure one tape signal edge on the EAR line (IN A,($FE) with row $7F), toggling the reference in C times out via B.
Used by the routine at tape_read_byte.
Out: CY=edge seen.
Clobbers: A,B,C.
i8080: ZX-tape/ROM specific -- replaced wholesale by the Specialist's own program-load path not ported.
